epicOrder.description: Authoritative implementation sequence for epics with open stories. Claude Code works the first epic in 'sequence' that has stories not yet Passed/Dropped/Deferred, unless the handoff prompt says otherwise. Maintained only via tdd-spec-session — implementation sessions never reorder this.

sequence.rationale: Listings must exist before the storefront has anything to show.

sequence.epic: Epic MFTF-13: Referenced Apparel Listings (Teemill Product Ref)
sequence.rationale: Second apparel sourcing mode (Teemill product ref). US-MFTF-13.1 additively extends the MFTF-5.1 schema and must land before MFTF-6 so browse/detail can render both designed and referenced listings from one normalized read-shape.

sequence.epic: Epic MFTF-6: Apparel Product Page & Browse
sequence.rationale: Buyers need browse and product pages, rendering BOTH sourcing modes (designed + referenced) via the normalized read-shape; the cart button ships stubbed here per US-MFTF-6.2. Depends on MFTF-13 schema.
DB-backed guest-capable cart; wires the MFTF-6.2 button and reworks print ordering (US-15.4) into add-to-cart.
Replaces Epic MFTF-7. Multi-line-item Stripe checkout, per-provider order splitting and fan-out. US-MFTF-12.5/12.6 close out as the webhook epic (MFTF-14) supplies live status detection.

sequence.epic: Epic MFTF-14: Provider Webhooks, Status Mapping & Lifecycle Emails
sequence.rationale: Makes provider status flow back automatically (Prodigi webhooks; Teemill webhook-or-polling fork) and sends the three buyer lifecycle emails (PRINTING/SHIPPED/DELIVERED). Retires the polling TODO in US-MFTF-12.6 and is the live-confirmation those two pending stories need.

sequence.epic: Epic MFTF-15: Seller Fulfillment for Originals
sequence.rationale: Hybrid split: physical-original fulfillment moves admin→seller (seller-scoped queue); admin retains a dropship-exception/retry queue re-homed from US-14.5. Dropship fulfillment stays fully automated. Depends on MFTF-14 for status/email plumbing.

Two small Passed-story revisions: remove Teemill from the designed-mode provider picker (enum retained, UI-blocked); default the first colour on the apparel detail page (size stays un-pre-selected). Independent of fulfillment; can land alongside MFTF-14/15.

sequence.epic: Epic MFTF-PF: Print Framing, Canvas Wrap & Seller Mockups
sequence.rationale: Print fidelity: seller-controlled per-aspect framing crop (canvas+paper) + canvas wrap (seller-fixed, MirrorWrap default, ImageWrap UI-blocked) + seller-uploaded per-size buyer mockups. Hard gate blocks ACTIVE until every offered aspect is framed AND every offered size has a mockup; strict archive-on-deploy for non-compliant live listings. Touches Passed US-15.2/15.4/15.6/15.7 and replaces the hardcoded fillPrintArea/no-wrap path in prodigi.ts. Independent of the fulfillment loop; slotted after MFTF-16 around the print work. Founder-ratified 2026-06-21.

Founder decision 2026-06-17, ratified in the 2026-06-18 spec session: sequenced after the fulfillment loop (MFTF-14/15) and before MFTF-10. MFTF-12 cart checkout shipped with Stripe automatic_tax disabled (STRIPE_TAX_ENABLED, default false) because Stripe Tax needs per-price tax_behavior + product tax categories + registration. Epic 5 wires that up and re-enables automatic_tax before launch.

sequence.epic: Epic MFTF-19: Storefront Polish & Pricing Visibility
sequence.rationale: Quality-of-life storefront polish + Teemill US-pricing visibility. Independent of the launch-critical path; 19.1 revises the Passed MFTF-6.2 carousel, 19.5/19.6 add founder curation tooling. Sequenced after core fulfillment, before the Pre-Launch Checklist.
Production readiness; largely human tasks (business registration, live keys, samples).
Open chores (CHORE-1 Vercel Pro/auction crons, CHORE-2 DNS, CHORE-4 live credentials, CHORE-16 commit-hash backfill) slot in around launch as needed.

Epic MFTF-18: Printful Integration (deferred — material-standard gate before scheduling)

stories.title: Designed product type can be created with a SKU that is not in Prodigi's catalog
stories.notes: Founder-reported 2026-06-21. createProductTypeAction/updateProductTypeAction (src/app/actions/admin/product-catalog.ts) accepted any non-empty providerSkuBase; the Prodigi auto-sync is best-effort (swallows errors) so a bogus SKU silently created a sizeless/colourless designed blank. Fix: added prodigiProductExists(sku) (src/lib/apparel/sync-prodigi.ts) — GET /products/{sku} is 200 for a real SKU, non-2xx (404) otherwise (live-verified 2026-06-21, see docs/prodigi-api-notes.md). A shared validateProdigiSku() guard runs in both actions before the prisma write: a 404 → 'No Prodigi product found for SKU "X"...' (rejected, nothing persisted); a transport error → 'Could not reach Prodigi to verify the SKU. Please try again.' (don't create an unverifiable type). Only reached for PRODIGI types (TEEMILL already rejected by US-MFTF-16.1, which is the only other designed provider). MSW: added a global GET /products/:sku handler (200 for any SKU) so existing create/update flows validate; the BUG-16 test overrides it per-test (known SKUs 200, others 404). Test: __tests__/mftf-16-storefront-corrections/BUG-16-reject-invalid-prodigi-sku.test.ts (create + update reject unknown SKU + no row mutation; valid SKU still creates).

stories.title: Cart checkout shipping form does not pre-fill the buyer's primary saved address
stories.notes: Founder-requested 2026-06-21. The originals/auction fulfill page (src/app/(main)/orders/[orderId]/fulfill/page.tsx) already pre-filled shipping from the buyer's default UserAddress, but the CART checkout (src/app/(main)/checkout) started blank. Added getDefaultShippingAddress(userId) (src/lib/account/address.ts) — the buyer's isDefault UserAddress mapped to FulfillmentShippingAddress (null when none; line2/state absence -> empty string), centralizing the lookup duplicated in the fulfill page + saveAddress action. checkout/page.tsx now passes it as CheckoutClient's initialAddress prop; CheckoutClient (src/components/CheckoutClient.tsx) seeds its address useState from initialAddress (falls back to the empty form, country 'US'). Buyer can still edit before calculating shipping. Tests: getDefaultShippingAddress mapping/null/non-default (node, mftf-12 suite) + CheckoutClient pre-fill render (jsdom, US-MFTF-12.3-checkout-client-prefill.test.tsx).

stories.title: Buy Now button active on a seller's own artwork listing
stories.notes: Founder-requested 2026-06-21. On the artwork detail page (src/app/(main)/artwork/[id]/page.tsx) the fixed-price Buy Now button was active even for the seller who owns the listing; clicking it hit initiateBuyNowAction which already rejects 'You cannot buy your own listing' (tested in US-15.1/US-2.3), but the live button was confusing. Fix: when isSeller (sessionUser.id === artwork.sellerId, already computed on the page) and not sold, render a disabled 'Your listing' button (title 'You can't buy your own listing') instead of the Buy Now form. Server-side guard unchanged (defence in depth). Auction bidding for own listings is unchanged (out of scope — request was Buy Now only). Test added to __tests__/epic-16-seller-ux/US-16.2-edit-listing-button-on-artwork-page.test.ts asserting the owner-gating predicate via getArtworkDetail.sellerId (same render-free pattern the page's other seller-gating uses).

stories.title: Teemill referenced apparel not orderable at 0 warehouse stock (print-on-demand ignored)
stories.notes: Reported 2026-06-21. Checkout 'calculate shipping' failed/pruned referenced Teemill sizes that had 0 warehouse stock, even though Teemill is print-on-demand. Root cause: orderability was derived from warehouse stock alone — ingest (src/lib/fulfillment/teemill/ingest.ts) set isOrderable = enabled && stock.level > 0, and checkout revalidate (src/lib/checkout/revalidate.ts) additionally gated on live warehouse stock (liveStock <= 0 -> removed). LIVE-VERIFIED 2026-06-21 against /catalog/products: every variant (incl. 0-warehouse-stock sizes) carries a gfnVariant (Garments-For-Nature on-demand print catalog entry, …/v1/gfn/catalog/variants/…) + a DTG 'front' applications entry, i.e. it is printable on demand; stock.level is only pre-printed warehouse inventory. Fix: isOrderable = product.enabled !== false && (hasGfnVariant || stockLevel > 0); revalidate now bases orderability on the live re-read's isOrderable (POD-aware), existence-checks the cached variant, and no longer prunes on raw warehouse stock. MSW fixture (__tests__/mocks/teemill-fixture.ts) now emits a gfnVariant per variant + a noGfn option for the genuinely-unavailable case; updated tests in mftf-13.2 (isOrderable from POD not stock), mftf-13.3 (publish-blocked needs noGfn), mftf-12.3 (0-stock POD kept; only neither-stock-nor-POD removed). Data backfill: re-ingested live products ('Don't let her burn.', 'Powered By Plants') so cached ReferencedVariant.isOrderable reflects POD. SEPARATE pre-existing issue found same session and fixed by data change (not code): a DESIGNED listing on a Teemill-provider product type ('Kiddo Tee'/RNC1) routed a Prodigi-shaped quote item (no variantRef) to the Teemill quoter -> 400 'variantRef required'; archived that listing + deactivated the 3 designed-Teemill product types (isActive:false). That contradiction is what US-MFTF-16.1 removes from the UI. // PENDING LIVE CONFIRMATION (same gate as US-MFTF-12.5/12.6): the shipping QUOTE step (POST /orders, no charge) exercising a 0-warehouse-stock POD variant, and whether the paid order (POST /orders/{id}/confirm) routes to POD via the catalog variantRef we send vs. needing the gfnVariantRef. Green vs MSW.

stories.title: Designed (Prodigi) product types have no colours — can't create a Prodigi designed listing
stories.notes: Reported 2026-06-17. Sibling of BUG-11: createProductTypeAction seeds ProductTypeColor rows only from teemillColorsJson, so PRODIGI product types had zero colours; the seller new-listing colour picker (productType.colors) was empty and publish stayed disabled (canSubmit requires offeredForType.size > 0). Fix: extended the Prodigi sync (renamed src/lib/apparel/sync-sizes.ts → sync-prodigi.ts; syncDesignedAttributesFromProdigi) to also pull COLOURS from /v4.0/products/{sku} and create ProductTypeColor rows ADDITIVELY (create missing by name only — ApparelListingColor holds a required, non-cascade FK to ProductTypeColor, so deleting would break referential integrity). Sync is per-product: 'Sync from Prodigi' button on the product EDIT page (syncProductTypeFromProdigiAction → syncDesignedProductTypeFromProdigi(id)) syncs that one type's sizes + colours, and createProductTypeAction AUTO-SYNCS once on creation of a PRODIGI type (best-effort; never fails creation). The bulk syncDesignedAttributesFromProdigi() is retained (cron-friendly) but no longer wired to UI. Name-only colours render as selectable buttons. COLOUR SHAPE CONFIRMED LIVE (2026-06-17): Prodigi product details expose colour as a lowercase NAME string per variant (variants[].attributes.color e.g. 'navy blue','natural','baby blue') with NO hex/code/swatch image of any kind (also confirmed sizes are lowercase 'xs'..'2xl'). To render colours as colours we derive an approximate hex from the name at read time (src/lib/apparel/color-hex.ts colorNameToHex; keyed to the live lowercase names) — applied in toColors (buyer page hex swatch), the admin product edit page, and the seller new-listing picker; unknown names fall back to a neutral chip, an uploaded colorImageUrl still wins. No schema change (hex derived, not stored). Folded into PR #11. NOTE: the test blank (Bella+Canvas 1010) is 52% cotton / 48% polyester — conflicts with the 100%-cotton brand principle; flagged to founder.

stories.title: Designed apparel listings offer no size options (Add to cart disabled)
stories.notes: Reported 2026-06-17. Designed (Prodigi) listings showed no sizes → Add to cart permanently disabled (referenced/Teemill unaffected). Cause: toSizes() (src/lib/apparel/detail.ts) read ProductType.sizes (ProductTypeSizeOption rows), which are only created by the admin add-size action; when size whitelisting was dropped that step went unused, so the list was empty. Fix: toSizes() reads ProductTypeSizeOption rows (honored if present) and falls back to an XS–XXL default (src/lib/apparel/sizes.ts) so every blank stays orderable. Per-blank truth is provider-sourced and AUTOMATED: syncDesignedSizesFromProdigi() (src/lib/apparel/sync-sizes.ts) enumerates ALL designed (PRODIGI) product types and GETs each blank's sizes from /v4.0/products/{sku} (Prodigi has no bulk-list endpoint, so we drive it from our own providerSkuBase values — no manual SKU list/paste), persisting them as ProductTypeSizeOption rows; exposed as a one-click admin 'Sync sizes' button (syncDesignedSizesAction on /admin/products) and reusable by a cron. // UNVERIFIED Prodigi size field paths (attributes.size / variant attributes) until a live run. No schema change/migration; fixes both the product page and checkout revalidation (shared getApparelListingDetail). Committed on feat/epic-mftf-12 per founder (folded into PR #11) though unrelated to MFTF-12. FOLLOW-UPS: (1) click 'Sync sizes' (or wire the cron) to populate real per-blank runs — default XS–XXL until then; confirm the Prodigi size field paths on first run; (2) pre-existing — designed fulfillment sends only providerSkuBase, so the chosen size/color are not yet encoded into the Prodigi SKU at order time (fanout.ts/revalidate.ts). FOLLOW-UP (2026-06-17): Prodigi sizes arrive lowercase + unordered ('2xl','l','m','s','xl') and were rendering raw. Added a canonical size enum (src/lib/apparel/sizes.ts: canonicalSizeLabel/sizeRank/normalizeSizes) — sync now stores the canonical label ('M','XXL') + raw providerSizeCode + rank order, and toSizes normalizes+sorts at read time so display is always standard and smallest→largest regardless of stored spelling/order. FOLLOW-UP 2 (2026-06-17): designed Prodigi shipping quote 400'd — Prodigi apparel requires per-item attributes {size,color} in RAW provider spelling + assets printArea 'front' (not 'default'); we sent neither. Fixed: ShippingQuoteItem carries attributes + printArea; revalidate resolves raw size from ProductTypeSizeOption.providerSizeCode (matched by canonical label) and raw colour from ProductTypeColor.providerColorCode, and sets printArea 'front'; Prodigi quoteShipping + createProviderOrder forward them (prints unchanged: no attributes, 'default' area). RESOLVED post-payment (2026-06-17): fanout toQuoteItem now resolves the same raw size/colour and attaches the listing's designImageUrl as the asset on the 'front' print area, so Prodigi createProviderOrder sends sku + attributes{size,color} + assets[{printArea:'front',url:design}] — the full designed→Prodigi path (quote → pay → order with the design on the product) works end-to-end. Test asserts the Prodigi order body. Closes the long-standing 'designed fulfillment only sends providerSkuBase' gap. // 'front' assumed for apparel (live-confirmed for the Bella 3001 blank); a blank printing elsewhere would surface via the captured Prodigi error.

stories.title: NavDropdown obscured by listing cards on browse page
stories.notes: The NavDropdown wrapper div (position:relative, no z-index) created no stacking context. Listing cards on the browse page create their own stacking contexts (via CSS transform or explicit z-index) and were painting above the dropdown menu. Fixed by adding z-50 to the wrapper div, which creates a stacking context that keeps the dropdown above all page content.

stories.title: Mobile menu toggle button shifts right on open; splash circle origin misaligned
stories.notes: Two related issues. (1) Button shifts right when menu opens because overflow:hidden or position:fixed scroll-lock on body caused layout reflow (scrollbar disappearance or iOS viewport resize) that moved the static-positioned button. Fixed by making the toggle button position:fixed at top:1rem / right:1.5rem (matching the header's py-4 / px-6 spacing) with a 44x44px in-flow spacer div to preserve header height. Reverted body scroll lock to simple overflow:hidden. (2) Splash circle emanated from top:1.25rem / right:1.25rem (viewport corner) instead of the button center. Fixed by updating the splash container origin to calc(1rem + 22px) / calc(1.5rem + 22px), which places it at the center of the 44px button (header padding + half button dimension).

stories.title: Mobile menu X button jumps position on open; circle expand animation missing
stories.notes: Two related issues. (1) Button position: using overflow:hidden on body causes iOS Safari to hide the URL bar and shift the visual viewport, making the static-positioned header appear to jump. Fixed with the standard iOS position:fixed scroll-lock technique: save scrollY, set body position:fixed + top:-scrollY + width:100%, restore all on close and call window.scrollTo() to preserve scroll position. (2) Animation: the previous BUG-7 double-rAF fix for the splash overlay still failed because React batches both the mount render and the splashVisible=true update into the same paint frame. Reverted to always-mounting the splash overlay inside sm:hidden (safe because sm:hidden=display:none on desktop, so no compositor-layer interference with NavDropdown). With the element always in the DOM at scale(0), the CSS transition fires correctly when isOpen changes to true.

stories.title: Mobile menu circle-expand animation missing on open after conditional-mount fix
stories.notes: Regression introduced when the splash overlay was changed from always-mounted to conditionally-mounted (splashMounted state). The element now enters the DOM with isOpen already true, so the transform starts at scale(1) immediately — no prior scale(0) state to transition from. Close animation was unaffected because the element is already at scale(1) in the DOM when isOpen goes false. Fix: added splashVisible state (separate from splashMounted). On open: element mounts at scale(0), then a double requestAnimationFrame defers setSplashVisible(true) by two paint frames so the browser renders the starting state before the transition fires. The circle-expand and circle-collapse animations are both driven by splashVisible.

stories.title: Sign-in hangs on loading screen after successful auth under --experimental-https on network IP
stories.notes: Reported 2026-06-11. Symptom: sign-in form shows 'Signing in…' indefinitely; refreshing lands on the correct dashboard (session was created server-side). Root cause: the mkcert certificate in certificates/localhost.pem covered only localhost/127.0.0.1/::1/0.0.0.0 — not 192.168.68.54. When accessing the dev server from the network at https://192.168.68.54:3000, browsers allow the initial HTML page load (user clicks through the cert warning) but silently block all subsequent JS-driven subresource requests including RSC payload fetches. After signInAction throws NEXT_REDIRECT, the client router tries to fetch the RSC payload for the destination route — that fetch is blocked, leaving useActionState pending:true forever. Fix: (1) regenerated the mkcert cert with mkcert -cert-file certificates/localhost.pem -key-file certificates/localhost-key.pem localhost 127.0.0.1 ::1 192.168.68.54; (2) updated the dev script in package.json to explicitly pass --experimental-https-key and --experimental-https-cert so Next.js uses the custom cert. Previous partial fix (direct-role redirect in signInAction to avoid the double-hop) is kept as a latency improvement.

stories.title: Buyer dashboard 'View →' link 404s — wrong route /listings instead of /artwork
stories.notes: Reported 2026-06-11. Both 'View →' links on the buyer dashboard (winning bids highlight and active bids list) used href /listings/${bid.listingId}. The route is /artwork/[id] keyed by artwork ID. Fix: added artworkId to BuyerActiveBid interface and query (selected artwork.id), changed both hrefs to /artwork/${bid.artworkId}.

stories.title: NavDropdown unclickable and sign-in hangs on network dev — mkcert cert missing 192.168.68.54 SAN
stories.notes: Reported 2026-06-11. NavDropdown button showed hover styles (CSS worked) but clicking gave no feedback in any browser (Chrome, Waterfox) when accessed via https://192.168.68.54:3000. Root cause: same as BUG-6 — mkcert cert did not list 192.168.68.54 as a Subject Alternative Name. Browsers load the initial HTML (user accepts the cert warning for the page) but block JS subresource fetches to an untrusted HTTPS origin. Without JS loading, React never hydrates, event handlers are never attached, and the dropdown cannot open. The 'sometimes works' behavior occurred on fresh page loads where the browser's cert exception was still active in memory. Fixed by the same cert regeneration as BUG-6. Earlier code-level fixes (conditional MobileMenu mounting, pointer-events:none on SVG, direct-role redirect in signInAction) remain as defensive improvements but were not the root cause.

stories.title: Auth redirects can send user to http:// instead of https://
stories.notes: Reported 2026-06-08. Fixed 2026-06-10 by correcting environment variables (NEXTAUTH_URL and/or NEXT_PUBLIC_BASE_URL updated to https:// scheme in Vercel env vars). No code change required.

stories.title: Fix image variant generation: tall/portrait images rotated sideways and cropped
stories.notes: Reported 2026-06-03. Portrait (tall/narrow) images come out rotated 90° in generated variants (display, grid, thumbnail). Fix 1: add .rotate() as first call in the normalizedBuffer Sharp pipeline (src/lib/artworks/variants.ts). Fix 2: use timestamped filenames for variant blobs so each generation produces a unique URL, defeating Vercel CDN and browser caching of the old rotated variants.

stories.title: Integrate Playwright real-browser E2E + visual snapshots
stories.notes: Stood up the real-browser E2E layer the founder wanted (jsdom/unit tests miss visual bugs). Fixed the stale playwright.config.ts (was http:// while the dev server is HTTPS): baseURL now follows NEXT_PUBLIC_BASE_URL (the app canonicalises host to it, so targeting localhost caused a cross-host redirect that broke the auth cookie), ignoreHTTPSErrors for the mkcert cert, a 'setup' project that signs in via the real form and saves storageState, serial workers (specs seed shared DB). First spec __tests__/e2e/buyer-order.spec.ts drives a seeded two-shipment cart order (Teemill apparel + Prodigi print) through the buyer order page and asserts both shipments render, tracking shows on SHIPPED, no provider names leak, and the rollup stays Processing until both ship — with 3 committed visual-snapshot baselines (shipments-processing/one-shipped/delivered). DB seed/verify runs in a tsx subprocess (__tests__/e2e/helpers/seed-cli.ts) because Playwright's ESM transform can't load the CommonJS generated Prisma client; helpers/db.ts shells out to it. Added tsx as a devDependency (was only resolved via npx). data-testid='order-shipments' added to OrderShipments for a stable locator. .gitignore excludes /test-results, /playwright-report, __tests__/e2e/.auth (storageState); snapshot baselines ARE committed. Run: npx playwright test (reuses a running dev server; first run on a new OS regenerates baselines via --update-snapshots). Vitest+MSW remains the logic layer; these add the visual/browser layer. Companion manual dev tooling: scripts/demo-mixed-order.ts (seeds a mixed Prodigi+Teemill order), scripts/advance-shipment.ts (drive any shipment by canonical status via the seam), and scripts/simulate-teemill-poll.ts (faithfully simulate a Teemill poll: raw Teemill status word → mapTeemillStatusToCanonical → transition+email, the symmetric counterpart to the Prodigi webhook curls). The demo drives the Teemill shipment via simulate-teemill-poll and the Prodigi shipment via webhook curls.

stories.title: Auto-Calculate Tax by Buyer Location
stories.notes: Stripe Tax (not TaxJar). automatic_tax wired on both checkout flows (createCartCheckout, legacy createCheckoutSession) behind STRIPE_TAX_ENABLED (default OFF pre-launch). Every line carries tax_behavior=exclusive + product tax_code (src/lib/tax/codes.ts: goods txcd_99999999, shipping txcd_92010001); billing_address_collection=required. Paid tax read back from session.total_details.amount_tax/breakdown and persisted to Order.taxAmount/taxRate/taxJurisdiction + true totalAmount (src/lib/payments/webhook.ts extractTaxInfo). Embedded Checkout shows the tax line pre-confirmation; Stripe receipt carries the breakdown; confirmation page shows subtotal/tax/total. docs/tax-configuration.md created (AC). // LIVE-CONFIRM: Stripe Dashboard tax registrations + origin address must be configured before setting STRIPE_TAX_ENABLED=true in prod; automatic_tax not yet exercised against the real account. Legacy src/lib/tax/calculate.ts (TaxJar) still present until US-5.2 removes it. Follow-up (post-PR #16): added logTaxDebug echo (webhook.ts, DROPSHIPPING_DEBUG) logging the billing address Stripe used + automatic_tax.status + amount_tax (later extended with customer_details.tax_exempt + per-jurisdiction taxability_reason + taxDestinationAddress; total_details.breakdown expanded on retrieve). Confirmed via live test calc that tax is DESTINATION/shipping-based: an Oregon ship-to correctly returns $0/not_subject_to_tax while WA returns standard_rated — codes (txcd_99999999/txcd_92010001) + registration verified working) for diagnosing $0 tax; docs/tax-configuration.md gained a Troubleshooting section (registration/origin/mode checks; the taxed address is the buyer's iframe billing address, not our shipping form). Made the default-off assertions (US-5.1 + US-MFTF-12.4) stub STRIPE_TAX_ENABLED explicitly so they hold even when a dev sets the flag in .env.local.

stories.title: Tax-Exempt Handling
stories.notes: Stripe-Tax model: exemption enforced by Stripe (Customer tax_exempt), not by zeroing a calc. Schema: new TaxExemptionCertificate (PENDING/APPROVED/REJECTED, exemptionType exempt|reverse), User.stripeCustomerId (buyer Customer, distinct from seller stripeAccountId), Order.taxExemptCertId. Buyer uploads cert (PDF/image) via /api/blob/certificate + TaxCertificateUploader on /buyer/settings → uploadTaxCertificateAction (PENDING). Admin approveTaxCertificateAction (src/app/actions/tax.ts) ensures a Stripe Customer (ensureBuyerStripeCustomer, src/lib/tax/customer.ts) and calls customers.update({tax_exempt}); rejectTaxCertificateAction → REJECTED. Checkout (createCartCheckout + legacy createCheckoutSession) attaches customer + customer_update when the buyer has one, and stamps taxExemptCertId on the order. isTaxExempt/getActiveCertificate read APPROVED (src/lib/tax/exemption.ts rewritten — old TaxJar-zeroing dropped). Admin approval queue helpers in src/lib/tax/approvals.ts (surfaced on /admin/tax in US-5.3). Removed TaxJar src/lib/tax/calculate.ts + MSW handler. // LIVE-CONFIRM: customers.update tax_exempt + exemption applied at a real automatic_tax checkout.

stories.title: Seller Tax Reporting
stories.notes: Reports live in the Stripe Dashboard (Tax > Reports, CSV) — not rebuilt in-app. New admin /admin/tax page (src/app/(main)/admin/tax/page.tsx, ADMIN-gated): (1) deep-link + how-to to dashboard.stripe.com/tax/reports; (2) nexus panel from getTaxRegistrations() (src/lib/tax/nexus.ts → stripe.tax.registrations.list) with copy that Stripe emails threshold alerts; (3) pending tax-exemption approval queue (US-5.2 approve/reject actions, src/lib/tax/approvals.ts). Linked from admin dashboard ('Tax & reports') + NavDropdown ('Tax'). Removed obsolete TaxJar in-app reporting src/lib/tax/reporting.ts. CONNECT/1099-K AC is MOOT (founder-confirmed): single-seller, no Stripe Connect (US-4.3 Deferred) — not built. // LIVE-CONFIRM: tax.registrations.list real payload shape + that Stripe nexus alert emails are enabled on the account.

stories.title: Multi-Currency Display
stories.notes: Display-only conversion (founder-confirmed): checkout still settles USD; listing/browse prices show buyer local currency with USD secondary. getBuyerCurrency precedence (src/lib/tax/buyer-currency.ts): account pref (loginMetadata.preferences.currency) -> mftf_currency cookie -> x-vercel-ip-country geo map -> USD. Pure resolveBuyerCurrency/currencyForCountry/localizedPrice in src/lib/tax/currency.ts (client-safe; no prisma). New ExchangeRate table + daily FX cron /api/cron/refresh-fx (Bearer CRON_SECRET, 0 5 * * * in vercel.json, Hobby-safe) upserting USD->{EUR,GBP,CAD,AUD,JPY} via refreshExchangeRates (src/lib/tax/fx.ts); getStoredRate reads cached rates (display reads DB, never live per-request). Currency selector in /buyer/settings (updateCurrencyPreferenceAction). Display threaded into ListingCard, ApparelListingCard, ApparelProductView via browse/shop/shop[id]/prints pages (getDisplayCurrency). plan.ts live getExchangeRate (shipping FX) untouched. Follow-up (post-PR #16): cart checkout now ALWAYS ensures a Stripe Customer and syncs the collected address onto it (ensureBuyerStripeCustomerWithAddress, src/lib/tax/customer.ts) + customer_update, so embedded Checkout pre-fills the address (no double entry) and Stripe Tax computes from the address we already have. Revised US-5.2 checkout-integration tests accordingly (customer now always attached, address synced). Follow-up 2: getDisplayCurrency now reads via getStoredRateOrSeed (src/lib/tax/fx.ts) which lazily fetches+upserts rates on a cache miss, so conversion works on a fresh dev DB rebuild and in the post-deploy window before the first cron run (was silently USD-only when ExchangeRate was empty); daily cron still keeps rates fresh. Follow-up 3: checkout order summary (CheckoutClient) shows a display-only 'Approx. in {currency}' line beneath 'Total before tax' converting the pre-tax total via the stored rate when the buyer's currency != USD, with a 'Your card is charged in USD' note — Stripe checkout/settlement stays USD. // NOTE: guests override only via geo/cookie (spec only requires account-preference override). Stripe documents the USD charge on its receipt.

stories.title: Print Catalog Filtered by Aspect Ratio
stories.notes: filterByAspectRatio() in listing.ts filters catalog to ±10% ratio match, sorted by closeness. // GUARDRAIL re Epic MFTF-PF (2026-06-21): Decision D keeps all aspect-matching sizes — this story's behavior is unchanged; re-run as a regression guard only. ENHANCEMENT (2026-06-22, founder-requested): the seller picker defaults to ONLY sizes within ±10% of the piece's aspect, with a 'Show all sizes' toggle that adds the rest of the Prodigi catalog. NOTE: the original filterByAspectRatio falls back to the WHOLE catalog when nothing matches (US-15.6 behavior, unchanged) — that made an unusual aspect (e.g. a 2:1 piece, which matches no catalog size) show every size by default. Added filterByAspectRatioStrict (src/lib/print/listing.ts): within-±10% matches (closest first) + saved-outside-ratio SKUs, and NO whole-catalog fallback (empty when nothing matches). PrintConfigForm now uses strict for the default view; displayCatalog = showAllSizes ? full catalog : strict; toggle shows whenever strict hides anything (incl. when strict is empty); empty-default renders a 'No standard sizes match… Show all sizes' hint. A non-matching size offered this way is framed via the PF.3 tool (crops the source to that aspect) and gated by PF.4. filterByAspectRatio itself unchanged (still used by nothing else; US-15.6 tests still green). Tests: print-config-show-all-sizes.test.tsx (jsdom, 4: matching-only default, toggle reveals all, toggle back, no-match→empty-default-then-reveal-all) + filterByAspectRatioStrict unit tests in US-15.6 (4: null→full, 4:5 matches only, no-match→EMPTY [vs filterByAspectRatio→full], saved-outside kept).

stories.title: T-Mill API discovery spike
stories.notes: Spike complete. API key obtained 2026-06-10 after 2FA issue was resolved with Teemill support. Findings: (1) Two APIs exist — Custom Product API (omnis/v3, creates Teemill-hosted storefront listing, NOT what we want) and Orders API (api.teemill.com/v1, correct target for MFTF-7). (2) Orders API is two-step: POST /orders returns available shipping options, then POST /orders/{id}/confirm selects method. (3) No sandbox environment — use MSW for automated tests. (4) Public product catalog endpoint (omnis/v3/product/options) returns 18 product types with colors and image URLs; used by MFTF-4 admin picker. (5) Colors identified by plain name strings ("White", "Navy Blue", etc). (6) Webhook payload still undocumented — stub in tests until confirmed from live integration. Full findings in /docs/teemill-api-notes.md.

stories.title: Create and Edit Product Type
stories.notes: 28 tests. 6 server actions in src/app/actions/admin/product-catalog.ts: createProductTypeAction, updateProductTypeAction, addProductTypeColorAction, toggleProductTypeColorAction, addProductTypeSizeAction, toggleProductTypeSizeAction. All auth-guarded (ADMIN only). Validation: required name/SKU, unique name, activation requires >=1 active color + size. Pages at /admin/products/new and /admin/products/[id]. — Enhancement (2026-06-11): Added Teemill product grid picker to /admin/products/new. Client component TeemillProductPicker fetches /api/teemill/catalog (route handler with 1h revalidation), displays a 2-col grid of all 18 Teemill products with per-card color carousel. Selecting a product collapses grid to single-product summary showing all color thumbnails. createProductTypeAction extended to parse teemillColorsJson hidden field and bulk-create ProductTypeColor records (backward compatible). Prodigi path shows manual SKU instructions. — UI revision (2026-06-11): Product detail page (/admin/products/[id]) redesigned: hero image from Teemill catalog; colors shown as read-only thumbnail grid (images fetched from Teemill, matched by color name); sizes section removed entirely (all provider sizes always available); activation guard updated to require at least one color (not active color, since toggling was removed). — Provider design audit + schema hardening (2026-06-11): (1) ProductTypeColor.colorHex made nullable — Teemill has no hex values; was storing empty string. (2) ProductTypeColor.colorImageUrl added — persists provider color photo URLs so pages no longer re-fetch Teemill API on every render. (3) ProductType.blankImageUrl added — admin-uploaded product blank photo for Prodigi products. (4) Blob upload route now accepts ADMIN role. (5) BlankImageUploader client component added. (6) Detail page shows BlankImageUploader for Prodigi; Teemill shows read-only API image. (7) Catalog list prefers stored colorImageUrl, falls back to Teemill API only when needed. Audit flagged 6 open design issues: provider routing by listingType not fulfillmentProvider, Order model has Prodigi-specific field names, isActive on colors/sizes is dead, OriginalListing.printProducts should FK to ProductType. These are deferred. — Schema cleanup (2026-06-12): Removed ProductTypeColor.colorHex (vestigial; superceded by colorImageUrl), removed ProductTypeColor.isActive and ProductTypeSizeOption.isActive (all provider colors/sizes always available — design decision). Renamed Order.prodigiSku→externalSku, Order.prodigiOrderId→externalOrderId, Transaction.prodigiFulfillmentCost→fulfillmentCost (provider-agnostic naming). Updated all 15+ source + test files referencing old field names. resetDatabase() updated to include new ApparelListing tables.

stories.epic: Epic MFTF-6: Apparel Product Page & Browse
stories.title: Apparel Product Detail Page
stories.notes: 20 tests passing (US-MFTF-6.2-apparel-detail-page.test.ts: 12 node read; US-MFTF-6.2-apparel-product-view.test.tsx: 8 jsdom component). getApparelListingDetail(listingId) in src/lib/apparel/detail.ts returns null for missing/ARCHIVED/SOLD (page renders 404 via notFound). Normalized ApparelDetail {id,title,description,retailPrice,images[{url,colorName}],colors[{name,hex,swatchImageUrl}],sizes[]} renders identically for both modes: designed colours = offered ApparelListingColor→ProductTypeColor (name + swatchImageUrl, hex null); referenced colours = distinct ReferencedVariant (name + colorHex, swatchImageUrl null) via referencedListingColors. Sizes: designed = ProductType.sizes by sortOrder; referenced = distinct ReferencedVariant in ingest order (referencedVariants ordered by id — cuid creation order — since there's no sortOrder column, so S/M/L/XL stay catalog-ordered, not alphabetical). Images: uploaded lifestyle photos (display variant, primary-first) else cached Teemill mockups (one per colour). No provider name/base price/currency/sourcingMode in the projection. ApparelProductView (client): plain- carousel + thumbnails, colour swatches (hex circle or swatch image — never branches on mode) with aria-pressed highlight + 'exact shade may vary slightly by batch' note, size selector (role=group, no pre-selection), and STUBBED 'Add to cart' button disabled until both colour+size chosen (real wiring is MFTF-11; selecting a colour does NOT change photos). Page /shop/[listingId] server-rendered with generateMetadata + OG image. NOTE: spec's 'Add to cart deferred to MFTF-7' is stale — MFTF-7 replaced by MFTF-11; button ships stubbed here per handoff. REVISED by US-MFTF-19.1 (2026-06-25): carousel assembly changed from lifestyle-REPLACES-mockups to the lifestyle-THEN-mockups UNION — toImages() now delegates to referencedListingCarousel; initial active slide = first lifestyle photo when present, else first mockup (ApparelProductView no longer jumps the initial slide to the pre-selected colour's mockup). 6.2 suite re-run green. POST-MERGE POLISH (ad-hoc): when the owning seller views their own listing detail page they now see an 'Edit this listing →' button (OwnerEditButton, gated by isListingOwner) linking to the edit page at any status — distinct from the existing unlisted-only OwnerUnlistedNotice. Extended: gating is now owner-OR-admin (canManageListing), and the artwork/print detail page gets the same top OwnerEditButton (replacing its old owner-only inline 'Edit listing' link). FOLLOW-UP: opened listing EDITING to admins (not just the owning seller). New src/lib/seller/authz.ts seam (getActor/getManagerActor + canManageListing) applied at every edit chokepoint: apparel referenced/designed actions (loadOwnedReferencedListing/loadOwnedListing), artwork actions (listings.ts requireManager on update/status/delete/print-config/framing/mockups; create stays SELLER-only), artwork image actions (images.ts), and both edit pages. Owner-OR-admin everywhere; non-owner non-admins still blocked; create flows unchanged. Designed so future role differentiation only changes the authz seam. Full suite green (1583). NEW (ad-hoc): buyer Discover homepage at /discover — a bento grid mixing ACTIVE apparel + buyable art (fixed-price originals, live auctions, print-available), reshuffled per load (force-dynamic). Pure toDiscoverTiles + shuffle in src/lib/discover/feed.ts (reuses getApparelListings + browseArtworks); presentational DiscoverBento; 'Discover' nav link. Intended to become the '/' landing later (today '/' routes to coming-soon). Refined: tile shapes are squares/portraits/2x2 features (dropped the flat wide tiles that looked bad); on hover a tile pops out of the grid (scale + raised z + shadow, smooth transition) and switches cover->contain so the whole piece is shown; the hover caption is a little info card showing title + price + a description excerpt (unfurls on hover, line-clamped). Popout image is top-aligned (object-top) so it butts against the top with no bar above it, the details sit in the space beneath, and the matte goes dark on hover (no white bar around the picture). REDESIGNED popout: theme-matched bg-surface card that hugs the image width (image natural w-auto/max-h, top-justified, no side bars); title + description in the open space beneath the image; a navigable multi-image carousel (feed tiles now carry the listing's full media set via optional ApparelCard/ArtworkCard.media, apparel incl. per-mockup backgrounds); mockup images keep their assigned background colour inline regardless of theme; smooth fade+scale in AND out (no object-fit jump). DiscoverBento is now a client component with per-tile carousel state. Refined: the details column uses w-0/min-w-full so a long title/description wraps to the image width instead of widening the card; the popout image has a minimum width of 420px (capped to 90vw on small screens) with auto height, so it scales up without distortion and is never tiny. Also added Discover to the mobile menu (was desktop-nav only). Surfaced an optional description on ApparelCard/ArtworkCard (already fetched via include) + a plain-text excerpt in the discover tile. The artwork/auction/print detail page (ImageLightbox) now renders the shared Carousel inline (matching apparel), while keeping its fullscreen lightbox + magnifier: Carousel gained optional onActivate (click image -> open lightbox) + enableKeyboard (disabled while the lightbox is open so they don't both navigate). Epic-19 lightbox suite stays green.

stories.title: Apparel Listing in Seller Dashboard
stories.notes: REMAINING SCOPE COMPLETED 2026-06-15 (6 tests passing, US-MFTF-6.3-apparel-in-seller-dashboard.test.ts): (1) Count summary — getSellerListingSummary (src/lib/dashboard/seller.ts) now groupBy-counts BOTH originalListing and apparelListing into one active/sold/archived/total summary (apparel covers designed + referenced; shared ListingStatus enum, counted identically). The /dashboard/seller summary cards now reflect apparel in totals automatically. (2) Badge refinement — the /seller/listings type badge is now colour-distinct per kind (Art = neutral stone pill; Apparel = cerulean pill) so apparel reads apart from artwork at a glance, not just by label. FOLLOW-UP 2026-06-15 (founder feedback): (a) the /dashboard/seller 'Active Listings' grid now includes apparel — getSellerActiveListings repurposed to the unified row shape (see US-13.2 note); (b) the /seller/listings header three buttons ('+ Apparel/Referenced/Artwork listing') were replaced with a single '+ New listing' button → a chooser page at /seller/listings/new presenting Artwork / Apparel / Referenced listing options, each with a short description (artwork = single original + optional prints; apparel = API-designed dropshipper whitelist e.g. Prodigi; referenced = build-in-provider-editor-then-link e.g. Teemill). The artwork creation form moved from /seller/listings/new to /seller/listings/new/artwork. (c) the apparel edit page (/seller/apparel/[listingId]/edit, both designed and referenced branches) now has a 'View listing →' button in the header linking to the public /shop/[listingId] page; shown only when the listing is ACTIVE since the public page 404s on non-active listings. The artwork edit page (/seller/listings/[id]/edit) gets the same 'View listing →' button linking to /artwork/[artworkId] — no status guard there, since the artwork detail page renders for any existing artwork (shows Sold/etc.) rather than 404ing on non-active listings. NEW STATUS 'UNLISTED' 2026-06-15 (founder request; 18 tests US-MFTF-6-unlisted-status.test.ts): added UNLISTED to the ListingStatus enum (shared by originalListing + apparelListing; pushed to both DBs). Semantics — visible only by direct link to the detail page, hidden from every feed, excluded from 'active' counts; a seller's pre-launch preview state, distinct from ARCHIVED (retired/hidden, not viewable). Apparel detail (getApparelListingDetail) now renders ACTIVE+UNLISTED (still 404s ARCHIVED/SOLD); artwork detail already rendered all statuses. Feeds exclude UNLISTED: apparel browse is ACTIVE-only already; browseArtworks gained a NOT-UNLISTED guard (covers print-available too); getSellerActiveListings filters ACTIVE. New seller controls: setListingStatusAction (artwork) + setApparelListingStatusAction (apparel) set ACTIVE/UNLISTED/ARCHIVED with ownership + SOLD + auction-bids guards and invalid-target rejection; the /seller/listings index replaced the binary Archive/Activate toggle with status-driven Publish/Unlist/Archive buttons + a violet 'Unlisted' badge. Apparel 'Save as draft' (designed + referenced) now creates UNLISTED instead of ARCHIVED so drafts are previewable (see US-MFTF-5.3 / US-MFTF-13.3 notes). getSellerListingSummary gained an 'unlisted' bucket + a dashboard stat card. NOTE: artwork creation still publishes ACTIVE directly (no draft); artwork reaches UNLISTED via the Unlist control. UNLISTED EDIT-PAGE REFINEMENTS 2026-06-15 (founder follow-up; 10 tests US-MFTF-6-listing-status-helpers.test.ts): extracted shared pure helpers to src/lib/seller/listing-status.ts (listingStatusStyle, listingStatusTransitions, isPubliclyViewable(kind,status) [artwork always viewable, apparel only ACTIVE/UNLISTED], publicListingHref). (a) The 'View listing' button on the apparel edit page now shows for UNLISTED too (was ACTIVE-only) via isPubliclyViewable — UNLISTED is exactly the viewable-by-link case. (b) New ListingStatusControls server component (Publish/Unlist/Archive forms via the setStatus actions + a violet 'unlisted — viewable by link but hidden from the store' notice) added to all three edit views (artwork, designed apparel, referenced apparel), so status is editable from the edit page. (c) The /seller/listings index thumbnail now links to the public product page (artwork → /artwork/[artworkId], apparel → /shop/[id]) when publicly viewable, falling back to the edit page for apparel that 404s publicly (ARCHIVED/SOLD). The index was refactored to consume the shared listing-status helpers (removing its local STATUS_STYLES/STATUS_TRANSITIONS copies). OWNER UNLISTED NOTICE ON PUBLIC PAGE 2026-06-15 (founder follow-up; +4 tests, 14 total in US-MFTF-6-listing-status-helpers.test.ts): the owning seller now also sees an unlisted notice on the *public* detail page of their own UNLISTED item (so previewing via the direct link is clearly flagged). Pure gate shouldShowOwnerUnlistedNotice(viewerId, sellerId, status) (UNLISTED && viewer===owner; buyers/anon/non-owners see nothing). Self-contained server component OwnerUnlistedNotice (does its own auth(), renders its own page-width container so it leaves no empty wrapper when null, with a 'Manage status →' link to the edit page) added atop both /shop/[listingId] (apparel) and /artwork/[id] (artwork). getApparelListingOwnership(listingId) added to src/lib/apparel/detail.ts to fetch sellerId+status server-side without putting sellerId on the buyer-facing ApparelDetail projection; the artwork page reuses the artwork.sellerId + originalListing.status it already loads. The unified index, archive/activate (toggleApparelListingStatusAction), delete (deleteApparelListingAction), and REFERENCED-row rendering were shipped early and remain unchanged (covered by US-MFTF-6.3-seller-listings-index.test.ts + US-MFTF-6.3-apparel-listing-lifecycle.test.ts). ORIGINAL EARLY-SHIP NOTE: Status was intentionally Not Started: MFTF-6 comes after MFTF-13 in epicOrder. However, one slice was shipped EARLY during MFTF-5 verification — apparel listings were not visible in the seller feed, which made it hard to verify MFTF-5 work, so the seller-index was extended to show apparel. Shipped early (do not rebuild): src/lib/seller/listings.ts getSellerListings (unified, kind-discriminated row list); the rewritten /seller/listings index (artwork + apparel); apparel archive/activate + delete; design preview. Still open for this story: dashboard count summary (overlaps US-MFTF-13.x referenced-listing counts) and badge refinements. Dependency: MFTF-13 will revisit this slice — getSellerListings must render REFERENCED listings, and US-MFTF-13.1 makes productTypeId/designImageUrl nullable + adds sourcingMode. Do not mark Passed until the remaining scope is built against the post-MFTF-13 schema.

stories.title: Teemill Catalog Ingest
stories.notes: 15 tests passing (US-MFTF-13.2-teemill-ingest.test.ts). src/lib/fulfillment/teemill/: client.ts (teemillGet + getTeemillProject — decodes JWT sub from TEEMILL_API_KEY, override via TEEMILL_PROJECT env) and ingest.ts. ingestTeemillProduct(productRef) calls GET /catalog/products with verified auth (Authorization = raw key, NO Bearer; ?project={JWT sub}=merchforthefuture-451391, NOT the public key — asserted in an auth test), matches the product by ref/id/slug, and returns a normalized snapshot {providerKey, providerProductRef, title, slug, providerBaseCurrency, providerBasePrice, variants[]}. Per variant: variantRef (absolute /catalog/variants/), colorName+colorHex (Colour attribute thumbnail), sizeLabel, stockLevel, isOrderable (enabled && stock>0), mockupUrl (matched from variant.images[] then product.images[] by variantIds). No 3-colour cap enforced. Errors (not-found/disabled/401/network) returned to caller, never thrown. No design file uploaded. applyTeemillSnapshot(listingId, snapshot, {preserveOrderableVariantRefs}) persists idempotently in a transaction (replace ReferencedVariant rows, preserve+mark-not-orderable vanished-but-ordered refs for 13.4, refresh providerBasePrice/currency/snapshotFetchedAt). MSW: shared __tests__/mocks/teemill-fixture.ts (verified Powered By Plants shape: 3 colours w/ hex, GBP prices, per-warehouse stock incl. a 0-stock variant, product-level mockups by variantIds, /catalog/variants/ refs) wired into global handlers. stories.title: Create Referenced Listing (Paste Teemill Ref)
stories.notes:
20 tests passing (US-MFTF-13.3-create-referenced-listing.test.ts: 15 node action/integration; US-MFTF-13.3-create-referenced-listing-form.test.tsx: 6 jsdom component, via // @vitest-environment jsdom docblock). src/app/actions/referenced-apparel.ts: resolveTeemillRefAction(ref) (auth-guarded; ingest → preview {title, providerBaseCurrency, providerBasePrice, colors[name+hex], sizes, mockups, orderableCount}; errors returned so the form re-surfaces guidance) and createReferencedListingAction (auth; validates title, retail≥$1, ref resolves, ≥1 orderable variant; creates ApparelListing sourcingMode=REFERENCED with productTypeId/designImageUrl null + providerKey/providerProductRef, no ApparelListingColor rows, no design upload; applyTeemillSnapshot caches variants+base price; redirects to edit). src/lib/apparel/referenced.ts: pure read-shape helpers referencedListingImages (lifestyle photos else distinct Teemill mockups), referencedListingColors, referencedListingSizes. Component NewReferencedListingForm: Step 1 'create on Teemill first' guidance + outbound designer link (new tab, rel=noopener) + how-to-find-the-ref blurb + Resolve→preview (swatches w/ hex, sizes, mockups, 'your cost, GBP'); Teemill named openly; margin hint is display-only with an illustrative rate (NO network FX). Page /seller/apparel/new-referenced + '+ Referenced listing' link on the seller index. MFTF-13 schema-touch of the early-shipped 6.3 slice: getSellerListings now renders REFERENCED rows (productType nullable — provider label 'Teemill', mockup thumbnail fallback, sourcingMode on the row); designed-mode readers (getApparelListingForEdit, updateApparelListingAction) made null-safe for the now-nullable productType. Regression: mftf-5 (53) + mftf-6 (39) green; tsc clean on src/. RESIDUAL: uses the same verified MSW catalog stub as 13.2 (one-product live-confirm flag carries through). ENHANCEMENT (2026-06-14): the description textarea auto-populates from the referenced product's Teemill description — ingest snapshot now carries the raw HTML description (TeemillProductSnapshot.description), resolveTeemillRefAction returns it cleaned to plain text via teemillDescriptionToText (src/lib/apparel/referenced.ts: strips tags, block tags/
→newlines, decodes common entities, collapses whitespace), and the form pre-fills the (still editable/clearable) description field on resolve. 80 mftf-13 tests green. HARDENING (2026-06-14): teemillDescriptionToText now drops

stories.epic: Epic MFTF-13: Referenced Apparel Listings (Teemill Product Ref)
stories.title: Edit / Re-Sync Referenced Listing
stories.notes: 19 tests passing (US-MFTF-13.4-edit-resync-referenced-listing.test.ts: 14 node; US-MFTF-13.4-edit-referenced-form.test.tsx: 5 jsdom). updateReferencedListingAction(listingId, _prev, formData): auth+ownership guard, SOLD read-only, edits title/description/retailPrice (min $1), refuses to change providerProductRef (immutable after creation). resyncReferencedListingAction(listingId): re-ingests providerProductRef, diffs cached vs new snapshot into a human-readable change summary (base-cost change £X→£Y, variant out of/back in stock, added, no-longer-available), applies snapshot via applyTeemillSnapshot with preserveOrderableVariantRefs = Order.externalSku for this listing so a vanished-but-ordered variant is kept and marked isOrderable:false (NOT deleted); unordered vanished variants are removed; returns {changes:[]} (empty = no changes). getReferencedListingForEdit(listingId) returns editable merchandising + read-only Teemill-owned data (colours+hex, sizes, per-variant stock) + editOnTeemillUrl; null for designed listings. EditReferencedListingForm: editable title/desc/price form + read-only colour swatches/sizes + 'Re-sync from Teemill' button (shows change summary) + 'Edit on Teemill' outbound link (new tab, rel=noopener). Edit page /seller/apparel/[listingId]/edit now branches on sourcingMode (referenced → new form; designed → unchanged). LIVE-CONFIRM RESOLVED 2026-06-13 (founder-confirmed): per-product editor URL is https://teemill.com/create-a-product/{slug}/?project={projectId} and the generic designer is https://teemill.com/create-a-product/?project={projectId}, where projectId = getTeemillProject() (JWT sub) and slug is cached at ingest on new column ApparelListing.providerProductSlug. teemillEditUrl()/teemillDesignerUrl() build these (3 URL unit tests); generic designer is the fallback when no slug is stored yet. Schema gained providerProductSlug (additive nullable, pushed to both DBs). The create form's 'Open the Teemill designer' button and the edit page's 'Edit on Teemill' link both use these. mftf-13 73 tests green; mftf-5/6 (70) regression green; tsc clean. ENHANCEMENT (2026-06-14): the referenced edit page now shows a central image carousel (ReferencedImageCarousel client component, plain since Teemill mockups are served from images.podos.io which is not in the next/image allowlist) populated by referencedListingCarousel() — uploaded lifestyle photos first (display variant preferred, else original, in sortOrder), then distinct per-colour Teemill mockups, deduped; getReferencedListingForEdit returns carouselImages and EditReferencedListingForm renders it centered above the merchandising form. 91 mftf-13 tests green; eslint + tsc clean. ENHANCEMENT (2026-06-14, US-MFTF-13.4 AC 'edit lifestyle photos'): the referenced edit page now manages lifestyle photos by reusing ApparelImageManager (add via Blob + corner-watermark pipeline, delete, set-primary). ApparelImageManager gained showDesignFile (default true; referenced passes false so the design-file uploader — designed-mode only — is hidden) and refreshOnChange (referenced passes true → router.refresh() after add/delete/set-primary/process so the central carousel re-reads server data). The carousel now leads with the primary lifestyle photo (then sortOrder, then mockups), making set-primary the cover/reorder control. The underlying image actions (addApparelImageAction/deleteApparelImageAction/setApparelPrimaryImageAction) were already listing-agnostic. 95 mftf-13 tests green; mftf-5/6 (70) regression green; eslint + tsc clean.

stories.notes: Dropped 2026-06-12 before implementation. Superseded by US-MFTF-12.6 (buyer order view with per-shipment status, tracking, and shipping emails).

stories.title: Cart & CartItem Schema
stories.notes: 31 tests passing. Added Cart (nullable-unique userId/guestToken), CartItem (polymorphic over CartItemKind APPAREL|PRINT), and the CartItemKind enum to prisma/schema.prisma; pushed to main + test DBs; added CartItem/Cart to resetDatabase TRUNCATE (before User/ApparelListing/OriginalListing). App-layer invariants in src/lib/cart/invariants.ts (exactly one of userId/guestToken; exactly one of apparelListingId/listingId matching itemKind; integer quantity >= 1), mirroring the US-MFTF-5.1 single-FK convention. Per-kind selection validators in src/lib/cart/validators.ts (APPAREL {colorId,sizeLabel}; PRINT {prodigiSku,attributes,quotedUnitPrice}) reject missing/extra keys and wrong types. NAMING NOTE: the spec calls the print-referenced model the artwork 'Listing'; the actual Prisma model is OriginalListing, so CartItem.listingId FKs OriginalListing. COLOUR-IDENTITY NOTE (deviation flagged per handoff): selection.colorId holds the normalized offered-colour identity = colour NAME (the value surfaced by getApparelListingDetail), NOT a ProductTypeColor FK, because referenced (Teemill) colours have no ProductTypeColor row; this is the cross-mode identity validated against the offered set in US-MFTF-11.2.

stories.title: Add Apparel to Cart
stories.notes: 23 tests (12 node action + 11 jsdom UI). addToCartAction (src/app/actions/cart.ts) validates listing is ACTIVE (UNLISTED/ARCHIVED/SOLD rejected via getApparelListingOwnership status check, since getApparelListingDetail intentionally also returns UNLISTED for preview), colour offered + size valid against the normalized read-shape (detail.colors[].name / detail.sizes) — never branches on sourcingMode/provider; referenced (Teemill) colours validate identically. Cart core src/lib/cart/cart.ts (pure DB, no next/headers — node-testable): findOrCreate{User,Guest}Cart, addItem with dedupe on (itemKind, listingRef, canonical selection) -> increment quantity + touch updatedAt, cartItemCount. Cookie I/O isolated in src/lib/cart/cookies.ts (httpOnly+secure+lax, 30d) and request resolution in src/lib/cart/request.ts (resolveCartForWrite mints+sets a guest token when absent; getCartCountForRequest is read-only for the nav). Nav cart icon + badge: CartBadge client component seeded by getCartCountForRequest in Nav (one element, visible desktop+mobile); ApparelProductView 'Add to cart' wired (disabled until colour+size) -> calls action -> router.refresh() updates the badge with no full reload, shows 'Added to cart'. Touched US-MFTF-6.2 component test to mock @/app/actions/cart + next/navigation (ApparelProductView now imports the server action); still green. UX TWEAK (post-review): selecting a colour now jumps the carousel to that colour's per-colour mockup when one exists (referenced listings carry one image per colour, tagged by colorName); lifestyle-photo listings have no colour-tagged images so the carousel stays put, and manual carousel navigation never changes the selected colour/size. Updated ApparelProductView + added 6.2 component tests.

stories.title: Add Print to Cart
stories.notes: 11 new tests + a US-15.4 add-to-cart assertion. addToCartAction gains a PRINT branch (src/app/actions/cart.ts): validates prints enabled + SKU in the seller-curated printProducts set (already aspect-ratio filtered per US-15.6, so membership IS the aspect-ratio check) + listing not ARCHIVED/CANCELLED. REVISED post-review (price-source fix): selection.quotedUnitPrice now snapshots the SELLER's printProducts[].price — the exact price the buyer saw on the artwork page — so the cart price matches the product page. The earlier live-Prodigi-quote approach was wrong: the Prodigi /quotes figure is a fulfilment COST, not the buyer's retail price; surfacing it made the cart show a different number than the page. src/lib/print/quote.ts + its MSW /quotes handler were removed (the authoritative cost re-quote/margin check belongs to checkout, MFTF-12, not buyer display). addItem now takes an optional dedupeSelection so identical prints dedupe on {prodigiSku,attributes} ignoring the volatile quotedUnitPrice. PrintOptionsSelector reworked: 'Order Print' -> 'Add to cart' (router.refresh updates the nav badge, no full reload); the sign-in gate was removed so guests can add prints (parity with US-MFTF-11.2). DELETED the direct single-item path src/app/actions/print.ts (createPrintOrderAction + /checkout/{orderId} redirect); nothing else imported it. NOTE: the src/lib/print/order.ts createPrintOrder lib is RETAINED (it is the underlying order creator used by checkout/fulfillment and by US-MFTF-3.2/US-8.3 tests) — those tests stay green. US-15.4 INTERPRETATION: 'artwork listing is active' is treated as 'prints enabled and listing not retired' rather than requiring the original to be unsold, preserving the print-independent-of-original principle. US-15.4 TEST TOUCHED: added a describe asserting the page flow now produces a PRINT CartItem with the quoted snapshot and creates no Order; all prior US-15.4 createPrintOrder/fulfillPayment tests unchanged and green.

stories.title: Cart Page
stories.notes: 14 tests (7 node + 7 jsdom). /cart server page at src/app/(main)/cart/page.tsx (force-dynamic; reads the visitor's cart via resolveCartForRead). getCartView (src/lib/cart/cart.ts) projects normalized line items: APPAREL title/retailPrice/thumbnail (grid variant, primary-first, referenced-mockup fallback) + 'colorId · sizeLabel' summary (no mode branching); PRINT artwork title/grid thumbnail + quotedUnitPrice snapshot + 'Material · Size' summary derived from the Prodigi SKU; subtotal + total itemCount. CartContents (client) renders rows with a quantity stepper (decrease disabled at qty 1), remove control, line totals, subtotal, 'shipping/tax at checkout' note, and 'Proceed to checkout' -> /checkout; mutations call updateCartItemAction/removeCartItemAction then router.refresh() (badge + totals stay in sync, no full reload). Empty state extracted to CartEmpty (links /shop + /browse). updateCartItemAction/removeCartItemAction enforce ownership via findOwnedItem against the visitor's resolved cart (foreign-cart manipulation rejected; quantity < 1 rejected; no-cart rejected). Added a /checkout placeholder page (404-safe 'coming soon'); the per-order /checkout/[orderId] route for originals/auctions is unrelated and untouched.

stories.title: Guest Cart Merge on Authentication
stories.notes: 6 tests (core merge + wiring). mergeGuestCartIntoUser(guestToken, userId) in src/lib/cart/cart.ts: union of items, summing quantities on identical (itemKind, listing ref, dedupe identity) by reusing addItem (so prints merge on SKU+attributes, ignoring the volatile quotedUnitPrice, exactly like add-to-cart); then deletes the guest cart row. Idempotent — replay finds no guest cart and is a no-op. WIRING DECISION (deviates from the plan's 'merge inside signIn/signUpAction before redirect'): merge runs in the NextAuth events.signIn callback (src/auth.ts) instead, because the AC says merge on a SUCCESSFUL login — events.signIn fires only after authorize() succeeds, so a wrong-password attempt never pollutes a victim's cart, and it covers BOTH credential login and signup (signUpAction calls signIn) with one hook, leaving the BUG-6-tuned redirect:true flow untouched. mergeGuestCartOnAuth(userId) lives in src/lib/cart/merge.ts (imports cookies+cart, NOT @/auth, to avoid an import cycle with auth.ts); reads the guest cookie, merges, clears the cookie. The event is best-effort (try/catch) so a cart failure never breaks auth. No change needed to signInAction/signUpAction.

stories.title: Guest Cart Cleanup Cron
stories.notes: 5 tests. cleanupStaleGuestCarts (src/lib/cart/cleanup.ts) bulk-deletes carts with guestToken set AND updatedAt < now-30d in a single deleteMany (no per-row loop; cascades CartItem) and never matches user carts (guestToken null). Route GET /api/cron/cleanup-carts mirrors the close-auctions Bearer CRON_SECRET guard (401 on missing/wrong token, 200 + {deleted} on success). vercel.json schedules it daily at '0 3 * * *' — Hobby-compatible (daily-or-slower); deliberately NOT sub-daily (that is CHORE-1 / Pro). Tests backdate Cart.updatedAt via raw SQL (it is @updatedAt-managed) and assert only the 31-day guest cart is removed while a 29-day guest cart and a 400-day USER cart survive.

stories.title: FulfillmentProvider Abstract Base Class
stories.notes: FulfillmentProvider is now an abstract class (was an interface) with a concrete fulfill() template method (validateJob → createProviderOrder → confirmProviderOrder) and new abstract quoteShipping()/checkFulfillmentStatus(); two-step confirm is a default-no-op hook Teemill overrides. Prodigi/Teemill extend it; the new MFTF-12 methods are stubbed (filled in 12.3/12.5/12.6). Factory returns base-class-typed instances; per-providerKey routing deferred to 12.3 (still routes by listingType). A subclass omitting any abstract method fails tsc — verified by a @ts-expect-error type-level fixture. Touches Passed US-MFTF-3.1–3.3: their test files were re-run green unchanged (51 tests across mftf-3 + 12.1). createFulfillmentOrder narrowed to Pick<FulfillmentProvider,'name'|'createOrder'> so Epic 14 US-14.8 structural mocks stay valid.

stories.title: Multi-Item Order Schema
stories.notes: Added OrderItem (reuses CartItemKind; apparelListingId/listingId exactly-one app-layer invariant; selection Json; unitPrice Decimal captured at checkout; nullable fulfillmentOrderId) and FulfillmentOrder (provider key, providerOrderId, status FulfillmentOrderStatus PENDING|SUBMITTED|CONFIRMED|SHIPPED|FAILED, shippingMethod/shippingCost, trackingNumber/carrier, notes). Order keeps its single-FK fields for legacy flows; cart checkouts use OrderItem rows and leave the single FKs null. Invariants in src/lib/orders/invariants.ts (validateOrderItemReference, validateOrderShape). Order cascade-deletes OrderItem/FulfillmentOrder. Applied via prisma db push to both DBs; resetDatabase truncates the new tables. Epic 14/22 order tests re-run green unchanged (legacy shape unaffected).

stories.title: Checkout Creation: Cart Revalidation & Per-Provider Shipping
stories.notes: Implemented + green against MSW (14 tests). createCheckoutAction re-validates each cart item server-side (designed: ACTIVE + offered colour/size + current retailPrice; referenced: ACTIVE + cached variant present & isOrderable + LIVE stock re-read via Teemill GET /catalog/products > 0 + current USD retailPrice; print: listing ACTIVE + availableForPrint + SKU present + current seller price), removes stale items with human-readable reasons, deletes them from the cart, flags print price drift, and pauses (summary.status='changed') for re-confirm. Groups kept items by resolved provider key (ProductType.fulfillmentProvider designed / ApparelListing.providerKey referenced / prodigi prints) and quotes shipping per group in parallel (10s budget) via provider.quoteShipping() — Teemill step-1 POST /orders (no confirm), Prodigi POST /quotes. Teemill GBP shipping is converted to USD via getExchangeRate (the single FX point, shipping only; item base never converted) per founder decision 2026-06-17. /checkout page requires auth (redirect w/ callbackUrl); collects address before payment (founder decision). HELD at non-Passed (Option B, founder decision 2026-06-17) pending a live Teemill proofing order to confirm: Open Q#7 (stable 'standard' shippingMethodId), Open Q#3 (rate limits — currently live stock re-read per referenced item, cached-snapshot fallback on failure), and whether an unconfirmed POST /orders ever expires/bills. All marked // UNVERIFIED in code. No live API calls in tests (MSW only). Stripe session + the 'Proceed to payment' wiring land in 12.4. The Option-B status string is registered in the US-9.6 VALID_STATUSES set and the /admin/tracker badge config (shared by 12.6). PASSED 2026-06-17: calculate-shipping is live-confirmed end-to-end against the real Teemill + Prodigi APIs (Teemill POST /orders step-1 quote with selectable methods; Prodigi /quotes with size/colour attributes + 'front' print area). Repeated live quotes create unconfirmed Teemill orders without any charge (the charge is at confirm, which is 12.5), so the unconfirmed-POST-/orders concern is resolved and Open Q#7 shipping-method shape is confirmed. Promoted from Option B to Passed. LIVE FIX (2026-06-17): first real Teemill call returned 400 on 'calculate shipping' — the quote request sent an empty contactInformation.email, which Teemill rejects. Fixed: the quote now sends teemillDefaultContact() (valid email, overridable via TEEMILL_CONTACT_EMAIL/PHONE; the buyer's real email is used at fulfillment). Also: teemillError() now captures Teemill's {message} body into the thrown error + server log (was discarded), and createCheckoutAction/createCartCheckoutSessionAction return a friendly {error} instead of throwing so the buyer sees a message. Confirms one of the // UNVERIFIED items (email required); the rest still need a proofing order. FOLLOW-UP (2026-06-17): live retry returned 400 'The contact information.email field is invalid' — the placeholder email was itself rejected (Teemill validates the address). Fixed properly by threading the authenticated buyer's real email into the quote (quoteShipping gained an optional QuoteContact; planCheckout/buildCheckoutSummary pass it; the actions pass session user.email; teemillDefaultContact remains only as a last-resort fallback). Fulfillment already used the buyer email. FOLLOW-UP 2 (2026-06-17): live mixed-cart (tee + print) surfaced two more 400s. (a) Teemill still 400'd 'email invalid' because the session lacked user.email so it fell back to the bogus placeholder domain; the contact fallback now reads DROPSHIPPING_CONTACT_EMAIL (the founder's single dropshipper-contact env var; TEEMILL_CONTACT_* kept as back-compat). (b) Prodigi /quotes 400'd because items omitted assets — fixed by sending attributes:{} + assets:[{printArea:'default'}] per item, and Prodigi's error body is now captured too. Regression tests assert the Prodigi quote sends assets and a Teemill 400 surfaces as {error}. FOLLOW-UP 3 (2026-06-17): Teemill quote parsed to $0 (suspected field-path mismatch vs the // UNVERIFIED shipping-methods shape, not a real free option). Hardened the parser: extractShippingAmount() tries totalPrice/price/cost/amount paths and returns null when none found (vs a genuine 0), and the raw response is logged whenever the amount is unresolved or DROPSHIPPING_DEBUG is set — to confirm the live shape before fixing the path. Awaiting a pasted raw response to finalize. RESOLVED (Open Q#7, live 2026-06-17): the raw response confirmed our parse path (totalPrice.amount) is correct — the £0 is GENUINE: on the wholesale Orders API shipping is bundled into the item cost (item totalPrice 15.63 GBP; order-level shippingPrice 0.00), so availableShippingMethods all return 0.00. Also confirmed: method ids are PER-ORDER UUIDs (no stable 'standard'), names are carrier services ('Spring Tracked'/'Store Collect'/'Spring USA'), and the whole response is GBP-only (no way to request USD — buyer total stays USD via our retail; the GBP wholesale incl. shipping is the deferred margin-watch concern). Code updated: chooseTeemillShippingMethod() excludes in-store 'Store Collect' and picks the cheapest shippable method; the method NAME is stored at quote time and re-resolved to the per-order id at confirm. Remaining for 12.3 'Passed': confirm an unconfirmed POST /orders doesn't expire/bill (still needs a real proofing order). ENHANCEMENT (founder-directed 2026-06-17): buyer-selectable shipping methods per shipment (extends 12.3/12.4 beyond their original 'default/standard method' + 'one shipping line per group' ACs — flag for a spec-session update of the Cart & Checkout Model). quoteShipping now returns options[] (deliverable only — in-store collect excluded); Prodigi omits shippingMethod so /quotes returns every service tier in one call (Budget/Standard/Express/Overnight); plan/summary carry per-group options (USD), cheapest pre-selected; CheckoutClient renders radios per shipment with a live total; the choice (keyed by shipment index, matched by stable method NAME) flows through createCartCheckoutSessionAction → planCheckout → the FulfillmentOrder.shippingMethod/cost and the Stripe shipping line. Tests: provider options (Teemill excl. collect; Prodigi multi-tier), summary exposes tiers, and the chosen tier persists to the FulfillmentOrder + order total.

stories.title: Multi-Line-Item Stripe Checkout Session
stories.notes: createCartCheckout builds one embedded Stripe session (Stripe Tax on via automatic_tax) with one line per cart item + one 'Shipment N — shipping' line per provider group (no provider names), after creating Order(listingType=CART, PENDING) + OrderItem rows (unitPrice captured) + per-provider FulfillmentOrder rows (PENDING, quoted shippingMethod/cost) in a transaction. Added CART to OrderListingType (cart orders leave single-FKs null; legacy if-chains have fallbacks; db push both DBs). Refactored checkout into plan.ts (planCheckout — internal grouping w/ provider keys + quote items) + summary.ts (summarizePlan/buildCheckoutSummary — buyer-facing, strips provider identity). createCartCheckoutSessionAction returns requiresConfirmation when the cart changed and !confirmed (no order created), else clientSecret. Webhook checkout.session.completed: runFulfillment CART branch marks PAID + Transaction + clears the buyer's cart (clearUserCart) + confirmation email; idempotent; fan-out is 12.5. CheckoutClient wires embedded payment (CartPaymentForm); minimal /orders/[orderId]/confirmation page (enriched in 12.6). Epic 21/14 regression green. DEVIATION (founder decision 2026-06-17, reaffirmed): the 'legacy single-item flows unchanged' criterion was intentionally relaxed — address-before-payment was retrofitted to the original/auction buy-now flow IN THIS BRANCH. Single-item checkout is now unified on /orders/[orderId]/fulfill which collects the shipping address BEFORE showing the Stripe payment step (needsShipping=!shippingConfirmed precedes needsPayment=shippingConfirmed&&!isPaid); /checkout/[orderId] forwards there (buy-now still redirects to /checkout/ so Epic 15 stays green); confirmShippingAction already supported PENDING orders so its tests are unchanged. Epic 14/15/21/22 regression green. NOTE: originals are founder-shipped (no dropshipper), so they have no POD shipping cost to capture; the destination-shipping-capture goal is met by the cart flow, so this retrofit is UX-consistency only for originals (shipping stays $0/manual). LIVE FIX (2026-06-17): 'Proceed to checkout' failed because every shipment emitted a Stripe line item including £0/$0 ones (real Teemill shipping is free/bundled) and Stripe rejects a $0 unit_amount line — a path the MSW-stubbed Stripe tests never exercised (they used non-zero shipping). Extracted buildCartLineItems(plan) (exported, unit-tested) which omits shipping lines with cost ≤ 0; item lines unchanged. Also shippingErrorMessage now surfaces the real error in non-production so checkout failures aren't masked by the friendly message. TAX DEFERRED (2026-06-17): the live session then 400'd on Stripe automatic_tax ('prices do not have a tax behavior set') — that's full Stripe Tax setup (per-price tax_behavior + product tax categories + tax registration), i.e. Epic 5 work. Per founder, deferred: automatic_tax is now gated off (STRIPE_TAX_ENABLED, default false) so checkout completes with no tax line (Order.taxAmount stays 0); the original 12.4 AC 'Stripe Tax on' is superseded pending Epic 5. Founder requested bumping Epic 5 up epicOrder.sequence — done 2026-06-17 at founder's explicit instruction. EMBEDDED UI MODE: this stack's Stripe (apiVersion …dahlia) names the embedded checkout UiMode 'embedded_page' (not 'embedded'; 'hosted_page' replaces 'hosted') — it's a valid literal here, so the `as any` cast was dropped. RESOLVED (2026-06-17): the full buyer path works in Stripe TEST mode — cart → calculate shipping → select per-shipment methods → embedded Stripe payment → /orders/[id]/confirmation. Diagnostic confirmed ui_mode 'embedded_page', status 'open', hasClientSecret true, 3 line items, $72.60. The earlier generic 'Something went wrong' (no client error) was a Stripe publishable/secret key mode-or-account mismatch in the local env, NOT a code defect; resolved once both keys were the same account + test mode. The session diagnostic log is retained, gated on DROPSHIPPING_DEBUG. BADGE FIX (2026-06-17): after checkout the cart emptied but the nav cart badge kept the old count until the next navigation — the layout's Nav (getCartCountForRequest) renders BEFORE the confirmation page's resolveSessionFulfillment clears the cart. Added <RefreshOnMount/> (a one-time router.refresh) to the confirmation page when the order is PAID, so the server Nav re-reads the now-empty cart and the badge updates immediately. jsdom test covers it. EMAIL FIX (2026-06-17): cart orders were sent the LEGACY single-item confirmation (sendPurchaseConfirmation) → showed 'Artwork', no item list, and totals that didn't add up (shipping line missing). Added sendCartPurchaseConfirmation: enumerated items with thumbnails + per-line totals, a Shipping line (sum of FulfillmentOrder.shippingCost, or 'Free'), correct subtotal+shipping+tax=total, and a label-formatted multi-line shipping address; no provider names; links to /buyer/orders/[id]. Webhook CART branch now calls it; tests assert items/thumbnails, the shipping line + math, and the multi-line address.

stories.title: Post-Payment Fulfillment Fan-Out
stories.notes: On PAID, the webhook CART branch calls dispatchOrderFulfillment(orderId) which dispatches each FulfillmentOrder through provider.fulfill() (the 12.1 template). Implemented the fulfill() steps: Prodigi createProviderOrder (multi-item POST /orders; single-step, default no-op confirm); Teemill two-step createProviderOrder (POST /orders) + confirmProviderOrder (POST /orders/{id}/confirm with the quoted shippingMethodId — // UNVERIFIED 'standard', Open Q#7). Per-shipment FAILURE ISOLATION: any error sets that FulfillmentOrder FAILED + notes and does not throw, so siblings proceed. IDEMPOTENT per FulfillmentOrder: skipped once providerOrderId is set (no duplicate provider orders on webhook replay). Admin retry: retryFulfillmentAction (admin-only) + a 'Failed shipments' section with a Retry button on /admin/fulfillment (US-14.5 page). Job items resolve referenced variantRef from the cached ReferencedVariant by colour+size, designed apparel to providerSkuBase, prints to prodigiSku + printSourceImageUrl. Legacy single-item PRINT webhook path left as-is (cart prints now route through the abstraction). MSW: Teemill POST /orders/:id/confirm + GET /orders/:ref added globally. MFTF-3 provider tests re-run green.
