stories.epic: Epic MFTF-14: Provider Webhooks, Status Mapping & Lifecycle Emails
stories.title: Provider Webhook Endpoint & Verification
stories.notes: Prodigi webhook route POST /api/webhooks/prodigi (src/app/api/webhooks/prodigi/route.ts). Public (no auth session); UNTRUSTED request authenticated before processing. AUTH MODEL CONFIRMED 2026-06-19 against Prodigi callback docs: Prodigi does NOT sign payloads and issues NO secret — you only register a callback URL and the body is unsigned CloudEvents. Secured by a PER-ORDER token (2026-06-20): at fan-out each Prodigi FulfillmentOrder mints an unguessable webhookToken and registers ${NEXT_PUBLIC_BASE_URL}/api/webhooks/prodigi?token=<webhookToken> as the order's callbackUrl; the route resolves the shipment by webhookToken alone (findFirst provider=prodigi+webhookToken) — a token matching no shipment → 401. No global dashboard URL, no shared-secret env var. Wins: leaked token = one order only; each environment self-addresses its own callbacks. Defence in depth: only enumerated events act. Schema: added FulfillmentOrder.webhookToken (String? @unique); db push to main+test. Handled event types are an enumerated constant HANDLED_PRODIGI_EVENTS (src/lib/fulfillment/providers/prodigi.ts); events outside the set are acked 200 + ignored (no transition, no retry-storm). Verified events are parsed by mapProdigiEventToStatus into the provider-agnostic {providerOrderId,status,tracking} shape and handed to the shared seam applyFulfillmentTransition (US-MFTF-14.2) — the route itself contains NO transition logic. Idempotent (replayed event → single transition via the seam's guarded write). NO Teemill webhook route — Teemill webhook support is unverified, so it stays on polling (GET /orders/{ref}); the '// TODO: replace Teemill polling with webhook once payload shape is confirmed live' marker is retained. 4 tests (valid sig → 200 + FO transitioned; invalid sig → 401 no-op; unknown event → 200 ignored; replay → single transition+email). // UNVERIFIED (no live callback this session): exact Prodigi signature header/scheme + event-type strings + payload field paths — documented in docs/prodigi-api-notes.md → Webhooks; confirm on first live dispatch. Env: PRODIGI_WEBHOOK_SECRET.

stories.epic: Epic MFTF-14: Provider Webhooks, Status Mapping & Lifecycle Emails
stories.title: Provider Status → Canonical FulfillmentOrder Mapping
stories.notes: Canonical mapping + the single FulfillmentOrder transition seam (src/lib/fulfillment/status.ts). SCHEMA: extended the FulfillmentOrderStatus enum to align with the canonical FulfillmentStatus set (US-MFTF-3.1) — added PRINTING, DELIVERED, CANCELLED (CONFIRMED reused as the PROCESSING base, FAILED == canonical ERROR); no new enum, no new table. prisma generate + db push to main + test DBs. Each provider owns its raw→canonical mapping inside the subclass so vocabulary never leaks: ProdigiFulfillmentProvider.checkFulfillmentStatus maps order.status.stage (mapProdigiStageToStatus: InProgress→PROCESSING, Complete/Dispatched→SHIPPED, Cancelled→CANCELLED, unknown→null+warn); TeemillFulfillmentProvider via mapTeemillStatusToCanonical (// UNVERIFIED raw values). FulfillmentStatusResult gained an optional canonical `status` field (additive; 12.1 fake unaffected). applyFulfillmentTransition(foId, canonical, {tracking}) is the ONE seam both paths use: canonicalToDbStatus reconciles; MONOTONIC forward guard (PENDING<SUBMITTED<CONFIRMED<PRINTING<SHIPPED<DELIVERED) — a stale earlier-status callback is logged + ignored (no regress); CANCELLED/FAILED are always-allowed terminal transitions from any non-terminal state and absorbing once terminal; unknown provider status = null = logged parse warning, NO transition. SHIPPED persists tracking+carrier. IDEMPOTENT via a guarded updateMany keyed on current status (count===1 = this call transitioned), reusing the 12.5 'row status is the guard' guarantee. checkAndSyncShipments (12.6 polling cron) refactored to feed this seam (now flows PRINTING/DELIVERED too, not just SHIPPED) — see founder flag below. aggregateOrderStatus treats DELIVERED as shipped-or-beyond; OrderShipments badge config gained PRINTING/DELIVERED/CANCELLED. 10 tests (canonical reconciliation, per-provider mapping incl. unknown→null+warn, monotonic no-regress, idempotency, terminal rules, Prodigi-poll + Teemill-poll drive identical transitions). FOUNDER FLAG: this rewired the US-MFTF-12.6 reconciliation code path — 12.6 status intentionally NOT changed (stays 'Tests Passing — pending live confirmation'; its flip is the founder's call after the live proofing order).

stories.epic: Epic MFTF-14: Provider Webhooks, Status Mapping & Lifecycle Emails
stories.title: Buyer Lifecycle Emails on Each Transition
stories.notes: Per-shipment buyer emails fired from applyFulfillmentTransition (US-MFTF-14.2) on transition INTO PRINTING/SHIPPED/DELIVERED (src/lib/payments/email.ts). Added sendShipmentPrintingEmail ('…is being printed') and sendShipmentDeliveredEmail ('…has been delivered'); sendShipmentShippedEmail refactored to share loadShipmentEmailContext and now subjects 'Shipment N of M is on its way!' with tracking number + carrier + a tracking link. Each email lists ONLY that shipment's items and labels it 'Shipment N of M' (single-shipment → 'Your order') — provider/dropshipper names NEVER appear. EXACTLY ONCE per transition: the seam's guarded write means the email only fires when count===1, so a replayed webhook/poll → at most one email per transition (idempotency reuses US-MFTF-12.5). The trigger path is identical whether detected via Prodigi webhook or Teemill polling. ON MAILERSEND FAILURE: the email is awaited with .catch → logged; the status transition is ALREADY persisted and is NEVER rolled back (order state is the source of truth). Automatic re-send is DEFERRED (project-description.md → Open Questions → 'Lifecycle email retry') — no test asserts a re-send. No 'order received' email here (covered by the post-checkout confirmation US-4.5/21.2/12.6). 6 tests (one email per transition w/ correct subject + tracking; no provider names; replayed SHIPPED → one email; two-shipment order → independent 'Shipment 1/2 of 2' emails; MailerSend 500 → transition persisted + failure logged, no re-send; per-item thumbnail+title+selection rendered). ENHANCEMENT (2026-06-20, founder-requested): each lifecycle email now renders a per-item row (thumbnail + title + colour·size/material·size + qty) so buyers can identify the products. Thumbnails are sourced ONLY from our own uploaded images (ApparelListingImage/ArtworkImage on Vercel Blob) — the provider mockup URL fallback is deliberately NOT used because those are hosted on the dropshipper domain (e.g. teemill.com) and would leak the provider into a buyer email, violating buyer-opacity; a referenced listing with no uploaded photo shows no thumbnail. Test asserts the our-domain thumbnail renders and the provider mockup URL does not.

stories.epic: Epic MFTF-15: Seller Fulfillment for Originals
stories.title: Seller Originals Fulfillment Queue
stories.notes: Seller-locked queue at /seller/fulfillment (requireVerifiedAuth + roles.includes('SELLER') else redirect '/'). KEY DESIGN DECISION (no schema change): physical originals are legacy single-listing Orders (listingType ORIGINAL, no OrderItem/FulfillmentOrder); to ship them through the SAME seam as dropship (US-MFTF-14.2 applyFulfillmentTransition + the 14.3 lifecycle emails) and to make US-MFTF-15.3 uniformity free, each original is represented by ONE FulfillmentOrder with provider='originals' (free-text provider field; status CONFIRMED; no providerOrderId; no OrderItem). ensureOriginalFulfillmentOrder(orderId) (src/lib/fulfillment/originals.ts) creates it idempotently; getSellerOriginalsQueue(sellerId) lists listingType=ORIGINAL + status=PAID + shippingLine1!=null + originalListing.artwork.sellerId match (dropship/CART never appear; once shipped Order leaves PAID and drops out = the 'not yet shipped' filter). New seller actions in src/app/actions/fulfillment.ts: markOriginalShippedAction (validates carrier+tracking, ownership-checked via loadOwnedOriginal, ensures FO -> applyFulfillmentTransition SHIPPED -> fires buyer SHIPPED email via 14.3 path, also sets Order.status=SHIPPED + tracking for the legacy /buyer/orders list, ORDER_SHIPPED notification) and markOriginalDeliveredAction (DELIVERED transition + email, ORDER_DELIVERED notification). loadShipmentEmailContext (src/lib/payments/email.ts) now falls back to the parent order's originalListing.artwork (our-domain ArtworkImage only, never a provider mockup) when a FulfillmentOrder has zero items, so originals still render a thumbnail+title in the lifecycle email. Nav: sellers get an 'Originals to ship' link. originals FO is never dispatched (dispatchOrderFulfillment is CART-only), never polled (checkAndSyncShipments requires providerOrderId), never FAILED -> never enters the admin exception queue (US-MFTF-15.2). 12 tests in __tests__/mftf-15-seller-fulfillment/US-MFTF-15.1-seller-originals-queue.test.ts (queue scoping/filters, ensure-idempotency, seller-locked ship/deliver actions firing the 14.3 emails, ownership + validation + non-seller redirect). NAV (2026-06-21, founder-requested): the seller nav item is 'Fulfillment' (was 'Originals to ship') with a count badge driven by countSellerOriginalsToShip(sellerId) (same filter as the queue) — a badge on the dropdown trigger AND on the highlighted Fulfillment menu item when count>0 (NavDropdown), plus a mobile equivalent (MobileMenu: a Fulfillment link — previously missing on mobile — + a badge on the hamburger). Nav.tsx computes the count for SELLER sessions and passes fulfillmentCount to both. The badge persists until all are shipped (a shipped order leaves PAID -> count drops). The admin nav item was renamed 'Fulfillment' -> 'Dropship exceptions' to disambiguate from the seller item for admin+seller founders and match the US-MFTF-15.2 page title; US-23.1/23.2 nav tests updated + badge tests added.

stories.epic: Epic MFTF-15: Seller Fulfillment for Originals
stories.title: Admin Dropship Exception Queue
stories.notes: Re-homed the admin /admin/fulfillment page (US-14.5) as a dropship EXCEPTION queue: removed the originals-shipping responsibility entirely (deleted the PAID-originals list + the markShippedAction ship forms) and kept only the FAILED FulfillmentOrder retry. New loader getDropshipExceptionQueue() (src/lib/fulfillment/admin.ts) returns FAILED rows with where provider != 'originals' (defensive: an originals FO never fails, but this guarantees physical originals can never appear here per the AC). Retry uses the existing admin-only retryFulfillmentAction -> retryFulfillmentOrder (US-MFTF-12.5): idempotent FAILED -> CONFIRMED via the providerOrderId guard, no duplicate provider order. REMOVED the now-dead admin markShippedAction from src/app/actions/fulfillment.ts (originals shipping moved to the seller in US-MFTF-15.1); its obsolete test __tests__/epic-14-fulfillment/US-14.5-admin-fulfillment.test.ts was deleted (subject re-homed). sendShippingNotificationEmail (the single-order originals email) is left defined but unused by app code. Added the three lifecycle-email exports to the US-14.1/14.2 fulfillment-page test mock because actions/fulfillment now reaches the 14.2 seam. 4 tests in __tests__/mftf-15-seller-fulfillment/US-MFTF-15.2-admin-exception-queue.test.ts (FAILED rows listed w/ error+buyer; originals never appear; admin retry FAILED->CONFIRMED idempotent; non-admin redirected). ENHANCEMENTS (2026-06-21, founder-requested): (1) Admin nav badge mirroring the seller one — countDropshipExceptions() (FAILED dropship FOs, originals excluded) drives a badge on the dropdown trigger + the highlighted 'Dropship exceptions' menu item (NavDropdown) and the mobile link + hamburger (MobileMenu). For admin+seller founders the single trigger/hamburger badge SUMS seller fulfillment + admin exceptions, while each menu item keeps its own specific count. Nav.tsx computes both counts per role and passes fulfillmentCount + exceptionCount. (2) Admin /admin/fulfillment page gained a read-only 'Originals awaiting seller shipment' oversight table (getOriginalsAwaitingSellerShipment(): all sellers' PAID address-confirmed originals with the responsible seller name, buyer, destination, date paid, amount) so operators can see what's pending and who owns it WITHOUT acting (sellers ship; data-testid='originals-oversight'). Tests: countDropshipExceptions (originals never counted) + getOriginalsAwaitingSellerShipment (per-seller attribution; dropship + shipped excluded) in the 15.2 suite; admin badge + admin+seller sum tests in US-23.1/23.2. NAV REFINEMENT (2026-06-21, founder-requested): admins get an admin-FOCUSED dropdown — the buyer/seller operational items (Orders, Listings, seller Fulfillment) are now suppressed when isAdmin (gated on !isAdmin in NavDropdown + MobileMenu; My Bids also hidden for admins per founder follow-up), and the admin item was renamed 'Dropship exceptions' -> 'Fulfillment' (still /admin/fulfillment). No collision because admins no longer see the seller Fulfillment item. The trigger/hamburger badge for an admin therefore reflects only adminPending (exceptionCount); sellerPending is zeroed when seller items are hidden. Non-admin sellers are unchanged (see Listings + seller Fulfillment with its badge). US-23.1/23.2 tests updated (admin label back to Fulfillment; new suppression tests; admin+seller trigger = exceptions only).

stories.epic: Epic MFTF-15: Seller Fulfillment for Originals
stories.title: Buyer Fulfillment Page Status Source Alignment
stories.notes: Uniformity falls out of the US-MFTF-15.1 decision (originals carry a FulfillmentOrder). The buyer order page (src/app/(main)/buyer/orders/[orderId]/page.tsx) now renders the per-shipment OrderShipments layout whenever getOrderShipmentsView returns non-null (i.e. the order has FulfillmentOrders) instead of only listingType===CART — so a seller-shipped original and a webhook-shipped dropship shipment render through the SAME component with identical status/tracking UI; the buyer never sees who shipped or how status was detected. getOrderShipmentsView (src/lib/checkout/shipments.ts) now includes the parent order.originalListing.artwork and, for a FulfillmentOrder with zero OrderItems (an original), synthesizes a single ShipmentLine from the artwork title; single-shipment orders are labelled 'Your order' (matching the email loader). Legacy single PRINT orders (no FulfillmentOrder rows) keep the legacy single-item layout. No change to the buyer-locked access control (getOrderShipmentsView still scopes by buyerId; another buyer gets null). 4 tests in __tests__/mftf-15-seller-fulfillment/US-MFTF-15.3-buyer-status-source.test.ts (original status derives from the seller transition; no seller identity or 'originals' key leaks; a seller-shipped original and a webhook-shipped dropship render byte-identical status badge + tracking line via renderToStaticMarkup; access control unchanged).

stories.epic: Epic MFTF-15: Seller Fulfillment for Originals
stories.title: Seller Sale Notification Email
stories.notes: Founder-requested 2026-06-21. When an original (fixed-price buy-now or auction win) is purchased, the SELLER is emailed that a sale needs shipping. sendSellerSaleNotificationEmail(orderId) (src/lib/payments/email.ts): to artwork.seller.email; subject 'You've sold "<title>" — time to ship it'; body has the artwork + thumbnail (our-domain ArtworkImage only — thumbnailUrl/gridUrl/url, never a provider mockup), the ship-to name + full address (shippingName/line1/line2/city/state/postal/country), the buyer name, and an 'Add tracking' button linking to ${BASE_URL}/seller/fulfillment. Address is guaranteed present because the originals fulfill page collects shipping BEFORE payment (US-MFTF-12.4 retrofit). 4 tests in __tests__/mftf-15-seller-fulfillment/US-MFTF-15.4-seller-sale-notification.test.ts (seller gets artwork+thumbnail+address+tracking link; sent to seller not buyer; no-op for non-ORIGINAL; fires from the fulfillPayment PAID transition). Follow-up: added sendSellerSaleNotificationEmail to the @/lib/payments/email vi.mock in US-15.1-buy-from-listing.test.ts (runFulfillment now calls it on an ORIGINAL sale, so partial email mocks on the fulfillment path must include it). // MailerSend domain caveat (carry-forward): EMAIL_FROM domain must be verified or sends are silently swallowed.

stories.epic: Epic MFTF-PF: Print Framing, Canvas Wrap & Seller Mockups
stories.title: Print Framing & Mockup Schema + Backfill
stories.notes: Per-aspect PrintFraming (artworkId, aspectRatio, wrap CanvasWrap? nullable — non-null canvas only, croppedUrl, normalized cropX/Y/W/H floats, needsReframe bool default false, @@unique([artworkId, aspectRatio])) + per-size PrintSizeMockup (artworkId, sizeSku, mockupUrl, @@unique([artworkId, sizeSku])) + CanvasWrap enum (BLACK|IMAGE_WRAP|MIRROR_WRAP|WHITE — all four modeled, picker constrains later). Both models added to Artwork relations + resetDatabase() TRUNCATE list (before Artwork). DONE 2026-06-21: schema applied via prisma db push to BOTH DBs (test DB pushed via DATABASE_URL=$DATABASE_URL_TEST override since prisma 7.8 config reads datasource.url from env, not --url). New lib src/lib/print/framing.ts: pure helpers aspectRatioKey (gcd-reduce, e.g. 8x10→'4:5', shared by 16x20), isCanvasSku (-CAN- vs -FAP-), aspectForProduct (parse size label, fallback static catalog by sku), offeredAspects (distinct aspect, isCanvas=true if ANY canvas sku offers it — PrintFraming keyed by aspect only, so one 4:5 crop serves canvas+paper; wrap applies canvas-only at fanout), offeredSizes (distinct skus). CRUD: getFramingForArtwork/upsertFraming (upsert on artworkId_aspectRatio, partial fields), getMockupsForArtwork/upsertSizeMockup/removeSizeMockup (upsert on artworkId_sizeSku). getPrintReadiness(artworkId): reads OriginalListing by artworkId (prints attach 1:1 via OriginalListing→Artwork, so keying new models by artworkId is consistent — reconciled the spec's artworkId keying), returns enabled + offered/framed/missing aspects + offered/mocked/missing sizes + ready (true when disabled OR all aspects complete [croppedUrl & !needsReframe] AND all sizes mocked) — gate predicate for PF.4. STRICT one-time backfill backfillPrintFramingArchive(): ACTIVE prints-enabled listings whose readiness !ready flip to ARCHIVED + upsert needsReframe=true framing rows for each missing aspect; compliant/disabled untouched; idempotent. Test __tests__/print-framing/US-MFTF-PF.1-framing-mockup-schema.test.ts (21 tests: pure helpers, canvas/paper round-trip, both @@unique constraints, readiness mixed canvas+paper incl needsReframe-as-missing + disabled-N/A, backfill archive/needsReframe/missing-mockup/compliant-untouched/disabled-untouched). stories.title: Seller Wrap Selection (Canvas Aspects)
stories.notes: DONE 2026-06-21. Wrap constants added to src/lib/print/framing.ts: SELECTABLE_CANVAS_WRAPS=[MIRROR_WRAP,BLACK,WHITE] (IMAGE_WRAP excluded — single app-layer source of truth for picker AND guard), DEFAULT_CANVAS_WRAP=MIRROR_WRAP, WRAP_LABELS (Mirror wrap/Black/White/Image wrap), WRAP_API_VALUE (enum→Prodigi PascalCase MirrorWrap/Black/White/ImageWrap, for PF.5), isSelectableWrap() guard. Server action setCanvasWrapAction(listingId, aspectRatio, wrap) in src/app/actions/listings.ts: requireSeller+ownership, isSelectableWrap guard (rejects IMAGE_WRAP/unknown server-side), verifies the aspect is an OFFERED CANVAS aspect (offeredAspects from listing.printProducts, isCanvas) else rejects (so paper-only/un-offered aspects can't get a wrap), upsertFraming(artworkId, aspectRatio, {wrap}) — persists independently of the crop (crop survives a wrap change, verified). Component src/components/CanvasWrapPicker.tsx: radiogroup of exactly the 3 selectable wraps, MirrorWrap pre-selected when initialWrap null, optimistic with rollback on action error. Composed by new src/components/PrintFramingPanel.tsx (per-offered-aspect cards; canvas aspects render the wrap picker; PF.3 crop tool + PF.4 banner compose into this panel later), wired into the listing edit page (computes offeredAspects + wrapByAspect from framing rows, only when availableForPrint). Tests: US-MFTF-PF.2-seller-wrap-selection.test.ts (node, 7: persists allowed, independent of crop, rejects ImageWrap/unknown/paper-aspect/un-offered-aspect/non-owner) + US-MFTF-PF.2-wrap-picker.test.tsx (jsdom, 3: exactly 3 options ImageWrap absent, MirrorWrap default, stored-wrap pre-select). REVISED US-15.7 — see its note; re-ran green. tsc baseline 16. FIX 2026-06-22: CanvasWrapPicker (use client) imported the wrap constants from @/lib/print/framing, which imports prisma from @/lib/db — that pulled the server Prisma client into the browser bundle and 500'd /seller/listings/[id]/edit (Turbopack 'chunking context does not support external modules: node:module'). Split the pure Prisma-free wrap constants into src/lib/print/canvas-wrap.ts (framing.ts re-exports them for server callers); CanvasWrapPicker now imports from the pure module. Verified with a clean production build (next build) — the edit route compiles. Tests pass unchanged (the bug never surfaced under vitest, which doesn't browser-bundle).

stories.epic: Epic MFTF-PF: Print Framing, Canvas Wrap & Seller Mockups
stories.title: Interactive Framing Tool (Canvas + Paper)
stories.notes: DONE 2026-06-21. SPLIT geometry from rendering. Pure geometry src/lib/print/crop-geometry.ts (DOM-free, every aspect-aware fn takes source imgW/imgH since a normalized rect's pixel aspect depends on image dims): parseAspect('4:5'→0.8 w/h), lockHeight/WidthToAspect, defaultCropRect (largest aspect-locked centered, height- vs width-limited by image ratio), clampRect (aspect-lock + MIN_CROP_FRACTION 0.05 + no-invert + bounds-clamp, width drives/height derived, falls back to height-driven if overflow), withWidth (resize), moveRect (bounds-clamped translate), toPixelRect/toNormalizedRect (round-trip), cropPixelAspect. Server crop generatePrintCrop(sourceUrl, rect, pathPrefix) added to src/lib/artworks/variants.ts: fetch → sharp().rotate().toColorspace('srgb') (EXIF first so rect maps to displayed pixels) → metadata for real dims → extract(left/top/width/height from rect, clamped to bounds) → jpeg q90 → Blob put (timestamped path) → url; NOT watermarked (production file). Server action confirmFramingAction(listingId, aspectRatio, rect) in actions/listings.ts: requireSeller+ownership, requires printSourceImageUrl, aspect must be offered, rect each ∈[0..1] & w/h>0, crops via generatePrintCrop to print-crops/{artworkId}/{aspect with : → x}, upsertFraming {croppedUrl, cropX/Y/W/H, needsReframe:false}; sharp/blob failure → {error} not crash. Component src/components/FramingTool.tsx: img + absolute crop-box overlay (shadow scrim), pointer move/resize handlers translate px deltas (÷ container size) → normalized via crop-geometry, clamps on pointer-up, reads natural dims on img load, pre-loads initialRect on re-open, posts via confirmFramingAction. Composed into PrintFramingPanel (per-aspect Framed/Needs reframe/Not framed status + Frame/Re-frame toggle); edit page passes printSourceUrl + per-aspect stored crop rect (from cropX/Y/W/H) + needsReframe. Tests: __tests__/print-framing/US-MFTF-PF.3-framing-tool.test.ts (node, 18: 11 geometry — parse, aspect-lock under resize, default-crop landscape/portrait, clamp out-of-bounds + min-size, move bounds, normalized↔pixel round-trip, produced-pixel-aspect within tol on odd dims; 7 server — crops+persists+clears needsReframe, blob path scoped by artwork+aspect, paper aspect works, rejects un-offered aspect/out-of-range rect/no-source/non-owner, sharp-failure→error). Playwright: __tests__/e2e/framing.spec.ts drives the real drag+resize in a seller session and asserts the 4:5 crop persists (server-truth via get-framing); added seller auth (auth.setup.ts 'authenticate as seller' → seller.json) + a chromium-seller PW project (testMatch /framing.spec.ts/; buyer project testIgnores it) + seed-cli ensure-seller/seed-framing-listing/get-framing/cleanup-artwork + helpers/db.ts wrappers. // Playwright requires the running HTTPS dev server (npx playwright test, separate from vitest) + loads an external picsum source image — NOT run in this session; vitest geometry+server crop fully cover the logic. tsc baseline 16. E2E FIX (2026-06-22): the framing Playwright spec flaked — crop-box mounts only after the source load event fires, and the seed used an external picsum URL (slow/blocked in headless → load never fired within the 5s default → element absent). Fixed deterministically: seedFramingListing now generates a self-contained 400×500 (4:5) JPEG via sharp and stores it as a data: URL (instant browser load, no race; Node fetch in confirmFramingAction's server crop handles data: natively — a same-origin mkcert-HTTPS static file would be rejected by Node's fetch, which is why a real-cert external URL was used originally). The spec also explicitly polls img.complete before asserting crop-box visibility. Spec passes (setup buyer+seller + chromium-seller). ENHANCEMENT (2026-06-22, founder-requested): crop-box ROTATION. A print SKU prints in either orientation, so a landscape piece must be frameable landscape on a portrait-named SKU (e.g. 17×11 art on GLOBAL-CAN/FAP-11X17 — the aspect filter already matches both ways via normalizedRatio max/min; verified). Added crop-geometry helpers invertAspect ('11:17'→'17:11'), variantForPixelAspect (pick orientation nearest an observed pixel aspect, log-distance), orientedAspect (orientation matching the source image). FramingTool now tracks activeAspect (nominal or inverted): on image load it defaults to orientedAspect(source) — or, re-opening a saved crop, variantForPixelAspect(rect) — and a '↻ Rotate crop' button flips orientation + re-defaults the box (hidden for square SKUs). All box geometry uses activeAspect; confirmFramingAction is still called with the NOMINAL aspectRatio so the PrintFraming row key (and fan-out lookup) is unchanged — only the croppedUrl's orientation differs. Tests: PF.3 geometry +3 (invertAspect, orientedAspect picks source orientation, variantForPixelAspect locks to a stored crop's orientation); US-15.6 +1 (landscape piece surfaces portrait SKUs). // UNVERIFIED: whether Prodigi auto-rotates a rotated croppedUrl onto a portrait SKU's print area or expects it pre-oriented — documented in docs/prodigi-api-notes.md; confirm on first live rotated order (if not auto-rotated, rotate the asset server-side before upload). tsc baseline 16.

stories.title: Publish/Activation Gate + Reframe Alerts
stories.notes: DONE 2026-06-21. HARD GATE in both artwork status actions (src/app/actions/listings.ts): setListingStatusAction + toggleListingStatusAction now return ActionResult (was void — void callers unaffected; ListingStatusControls/seller listings page ignore the value) and, when the target is ACTIVE and listing.availableForPrint, call getPrintReadiness(artworkId); if !ready they return itemizePrintReadiness(readiness, products) WITHOUT mutating status. Itemized (not generic): 'Can't publish this print listing yet — add framing for aspect 4:5, 2:3; and a buyer mockup for 12×18 in.' (sizes shown via offered labels). Non-ACTIVE targets (UNLISTED/ARCHIVED) and prints-disabled listings are never gated. getPrintReadiness extended with needsReframeAspects (additive; PF.1 tests still green). Advisory banner src/components/PrintReadinessBanner.tsx (role=alert, pure/server-renderable): enumerates never-framed aspects (frame it → #print-framing), needs-reframe aspects (source art changed → reframe it), and missing-mockup sizes by label (add one → #print-config); renders null when disabled or ready. Wired into the edit page above the form (passes getPrintReadiness + sku→label map); added id anchors print-config/print-framing. Decision E art-replace in updatePrintConfigAction: when printSourceImageUrl changes from a prior non-null value, invalidateFramingForArtwork(artworkId) (clears croppedUrl+rect, sets needsReframe=true on all rows) and demotes an ACTIVE listing → UNLISTED (forced out of active-eligible until reframed; re-activation then blocked by the gate). No invalidation when the source URL is unchanged. Tests: US-MFTF-PF.4-publish-gate.test.ts (node, 10: blocks unframed/missing-mockup/needsReframe-with-crop incl status unchanged + itemized text; allows when complete; toggle gated; UNLISTED/ARCHIVED never gated; prints-disabled ungated; art-replace clears+demotes+blocks-reactivate; no-op on unchanged source) + US-MFTF-PF.4-readiness-banner.test.tsx (jsdom, 5: enumerates unframed w/ fix link, distinguishes needs-reframe, lists missing sizes by label, null when ready, null when disabled). TOUCHED listing status transition — re-ran US-9.5 (archive/activate), US-11.2 (deactivate), US-MFTF-6 unlisted-status: all green (33), non-print transitions unchanged. Re-ran US-15.6 guardrail (unchanged, Decision D) green. tsc baseline 16.

stories.title: Fan-Out Sends Wrap + Cropped Asset to Prodigi
stories.notes: DONE 2026-06-21. Shared resolver resolvePrintFanout({artworkId, sku, sizeLabel, fallbackSourceUrl}) in src/lib/print/framing.ts: derives aspect (aspectForProduct) + canvas-ness (isCanvasSku), looks up PrintFraming[artworkId,aspect]; if croppedUrl → {sourceImageUrl:croppedUrl, attributes: canvas?{wrap:WRAP_API_VALUE[row.wrap??MIRROR_WRAP]}:{}, framed:true}; else DEFENSIVE fallback {sourceImageUrl:fallback original, attributes: canvas?{wrap:'MirrorWrap'}:{}, framed:false} + console.warn('[print-fanout] ANOMALY...'). ShippingQuoteItem gained framed?:boolean; wrap rides in attributes (so it appears on BOTH order + quote — parity, no separate quote change needed since quoteShipping already forwards attributes). prodigi.ts createProviderOrder: sizing = framed?'fitPrintArea':'fillPrintArea' (was hardcoded fillPrintArea); attributes only spread when non-empty (paper sends none); asset url unchanged mechanism. Wired resolver into BOTH item builders: checkout/fanout.ts toQuoteItem (now async; PRINT branch resolves via originalListing.artworkId+printProducts size label; Promise.all in processFulfillmentOrder; foInclude now selects artworkId+printProducts) and checkout/revalidate.ts (PRINT kept branch resolves for quote-time parity; originalListing select gained artworkId). CHOSE sizing='fitPrintArea' for framed items (exact-aspect crop → show whole confirmed crop, no face re-crop); apparel + fallback keep fillPrintArea; documented in docs/prodigi-api-notes.md (replaced the 'unscoped feature work' note with the implemented contract). Test __tests__/print-framing/US-MFTF-PF.5-prodigi-fanout.test.ts (8: resolver canvas-wrap/default-MirrorWrap/paper-no-wrap/anomaly-fallback; order body canvas fitPrintArea+wrap+croppedUrl asset, paper fitPrintArea+no-attributes+croppedUrl, apparel UNCHANGED fillPrintArea+size/color; quote canvas attributes.wrap parity — exact bodies via MSW server.use capture). Re-ran US-MFTF-3.2 prodigi-provider + US-MFTF-12.3 revalidation-shipping (37) + US-MFTF-12.5 fanout (5, its pre-PF unframed print seed now exercises the defensive fallback + anomaly log — expected): all green, apparel/other paths unchanged. (No US-8.3 suite exists; epic-8 print-shop tests not affected.) tsc baseline 16. // STILL UNVERIFIED against a real paid order — sandbox-verified only (ord_1161006/1161007); confirm wrap+fitPrintArea+croppedUrl on first live paid canvas order.

stories.title: Seller Per-Size Mockup Upload
stories.notes: DONE 2026-06-21. Server actions setSizeMockupAction(listingId, sizeSku, mockupUrl) + removeSizeMockupAction(listingId, sizeSku) in actions/listings.ts: requireSeller+ownership, set validates mockupUrl is http(s) + sizeSku is an offered size (in printProducts) then upsertSizeMockup; remove → removeSizeMockup. Component src/components/SizeMockupUploader.tsx: one control per offered size (label + thumb/Replace/Remove or '+ Add mockup image'); uploads client-side via @vercel/blob/client upload() → /api/blob/upload (same pipeline that validates type/size; accept=image/jpeg,png,webp) then persists the returned URL via setSizeMockupAction; optimistic state. Wired into edit page as a 'Buyer mockups' section (id=print-mockups; PF.4 banner missing-mockup links now point here) fed by getMockupsForArtwork + offeredSizes. WATERMARK DECISION (was open in spec US-PF.6): FOUNDER CONFIRMED 2026-06-22 → mode = CORNER brand mark (the subtle bottom-right 'MFTF' mark, same as apparel lifestyle photos — NOT the diagonal original-protection overlay). Implemented via new generateWatermarkedMockup(sourceUrl, pathPrefix) in src/lib/artworks/variants.ts (normalize→resize 2400 inside→composite buildCornerWatermarkSvg→jpeg q85→Blob put, single output not the 3-variant set); setSizeMockupAction now runs the client-uploaded raw image through it server-side and stores the WATERMARKED Blob URL (raw upload orphaned, harmless). Rationale: brand identification without degrading the promotional image. Sharp/Blob failure → {error}, nothing persisted. Test asserts the composited SVG contains 'MFTF' (not 'Merch for the Future') + the stored URL is the watermarked output (not the raw upload). MIGRATION: removed the now-duplicate inline per-SKU mockup upload from PrintConfigForm (SkuState/SavedProduct dropped mockupUrl; form no longer writes printProducts[].mockupUrl) — PrintSizeMockup is the single source of truth. Pre-existing inline mockups are NOT migrated (the PF.1 strict backfill archives non-compliant ACTIVE listings on deploy, so sellers re-supply via the new control — consistent with the gate). Tests: US-MFTF-PF.6-seller-mockup-upload.test.ts (node, 7: corner-watermarked output stored [MFTF mark, not raw upload], replace overwrites, remove clears + gate reports missing, watermark-failure→error+nothing-stored, rejects empty/non-URL, rejects un-offered size, rejects non-owner set+remove; sharp+blob+fetch mocked) + US-MFTF-PF.6-mockup-uploader.test.tsx (jsdom, 3: one control/size, existing shows preview+Replace/Remove, Remove calls action + reverts to add state). REVISED Passed US-15.4 — see its note; re-ran US-15.4 order suite green (order/checkout path unchanged). tsc baseline 16.

stories.epic: Epic MFTF-19: Storefront Polish & Pricing Visibility
stories.title: Lifestyle Photos Lead, Mockups Follow in Apparel Carousel
stories.notes: Revises Passed US-MFTF-6.2 carousel assembly: change from 'lifestyle-replaces-mockups' to a 'lifestyle-then-mockups' UNION. Carousel renders all lifestyle photos (stored order) then all mockups (stored order); first active slide = first lifestyle photo if any, else first mockup. Mockups-only and lifestyle-only cases preserved. Identical for DESIGNED and REFERENCED — carousel reads the normalized media projection, does NOT branch on sourcingMode/provider; REFERENCED mockups come from cached Teemill images[].src, DESIGNED from existing source, both feed one ordered media[] list. No schema change; introduces a single ordered media[] projection as carousel input if one doesn't already exist. Test the media-assembly selector as a pure fn (mockups-only / lifestyle-only / both / within-group order) + component test for first active slide and full sequence. POST-MERGE POLISH (ad-hoc, no story): the carousel main viewer is now a fixed aspect-square box with the image letterboxed via object-contain, so it stays a constant size as you cycle (was resizing to each image's dimensions) — ApparelProductView + seller ReferencedImageCarousel. The letterbox area is transparent (page shows through) — removed the default bg-stone-100 so only a mockup's US-19.7 seller-chosen background (inline) fills the frame; lifestyle photos no longer show grey bars. Left/Right arrow keys now cycle the buyer listing-page carousel (wraparound), ignored while typing in a form field; manual nav doesn't change selected colour/size. REFACTOR: extracted a shared src/components/Carousel.tsx (fixed aspect-square frame, transparent letterbox, US-19.7 background compositing, thumbnails, on-screen arrows + keyboard nav). Buyer ApparelProductView renders it controlled (index/onIndexChange) so colour-select still jumps to the colour's mockup; seller ReferencedImageCarousel is now a thin adapter that maps the referenced read-shape (+ Teemill-mockup caption). Removes the duplicated buyer/seller carousels that had drifted (buyer lacked on-screen arrows; seller lacked keyboard nav); both now consistent. New shared-carousel.test.tsx; existing consumer tests stay green. Carousel nav arrows restyled subtle: transparent button (padding keeps the click target) with a white < / > SVG chevron + soft dark drop-shadow so the white stroke stays legible on any background. Carousel frame default is 5:4 (was square): with object-contain + transparent letterbox + overflow-hidden rounded corners, a 5:4 photo fills the box and reveals the rounded corners, while a square photo is pillarboxed so its rounding sits in the transparent bars (corners not revealed).

Each story row shows testPassedDate when present, clear empty state otherwise. stories.epic: Epic MFTF-19: Storefront Polish & Pricing Visibility
stories.title: Dark Mode Theme Tokens
stories.notes: Infrastructure story (toggle is 19.4). Define a complete dark-theme token set alongside light tokens in globals.css within @theme inline {} (Tailwind v4, NO config file) covering background, surface, text, border, brand accent(s) incl. a night-appropriate treatment of the current yellow accent. Switch via a single root mechanism (data-theme/.dark on <html>); all component colors resolve through tokens — no hard-coded hex outside token defs. Dark contrast meets WCAG AA for body text + interactive controls. Media (watermarked imagery, product photos) unaffected — theme governs chrome only. No regression to light theme (remains rendered default until 19.4 resolution runs). Color assertions are weak in unit tests: smoke-test that .dark root resolves a known token to its dark value + a manual-QA contrast checklist note. Flag any criterion not auto-testable. IMPLEMENTED 2026-06-25: semantic tokens (--color-bg/surface/text/muted/border/accent/accent-contrast) registered in @theme inline as var() refs; light values on :root, dark overrides under .dark / [data-theme="dark"] (single root mechanism) + @custom-variant dark + color-scheme. Night-appropriate accent #fcd968. Body migrated to bg-bg/text-text with light --bg matching the prior tuscan-sun/8 tint (no light regression). FLAGGED (not auto-tested): WCAG-AA contrast + media-unaffected are manual QA; full per-component palette (stone/gray) migration beyond core chrome is follow-up. POST-MERGE POLISH (ad-hoc, no story): (a) named --color-cream #fefbf1 + --color-bar-dark #1a1a1a; dark-mode top bar is now dark grey with cream text (Nav dark: variants). (b) Did the flagged follow-up as a single CSS palette remap under :is(.dark,[data-theme=dark]) in globals.css that maps the neutral Tailwind utilities (bg-white/stone/gray/neutral, text-, border-, divide-, placeholder, hover:) to the dark tokens so ALL pages flip at once, leaving brand + status colors untouched. (c) Retuned dark tokens to three tiers (bar #1a1a1a < page --bg #2a2a2a < surface #383838) + cream text + control greys. (d) Routed the 3 dashboards' min-h-screen bg-white page roots to bg-bg so page/card contrast survives the remap.

stories.title: Dark Mode Toggle with OS-Aware Persistence
stories.notes: Depends on 19.3 (tokens must exist to flip). First visit, no stored pref => follow OS prefers-color-scheme. Visible toggle switches light/dark; choice persists across navigation + future visits. Persistence readable during SSR so correct theme renders on INITIAL PAINT WITH NO FLASH (App Router) => cookie-based, not localStorage-only. Persisted manual choice precedence > OS; OS governs only when no manual choice stored. Toggle keyboard-operable w/ accessible label + pressed/checked state. Works for authed + guest (no account). Spec resolution as reading cookie server-side and setting root attribute before hydration. Test resolver precedence (cookie > OS > light default) as pure fn; component test toggle flips root attr + writes cookie + accessible name/role. No DB persistence — cookie matches existing guest-cart cookie pattern.

stories.title: Founder Records US-Landed Cost per Teemill Product
stories.notes: MANUAL data-source MVP with a seam for later automation (founder eyeballs US cost the way they do now; automated POST /orders quoting deferred — blocked by Open Q#3 rate limits + UNVERIFIED whether an unconfirmed POST /orders expires/bills, and the £0 bundled-shipping quirk). Referenced (Teemill) listing/catalog entry stores a founder-entered US-landed cost as USD (nullable; null = not recorded). Editable by founder (seller/admin) from referenced-listing context; validates non-negative currency. INDEPENDENT of providerBasePrice (cached GBP) and retailPrice — a curation datum, NOT used in any checkout/pricing/margin/sticker calc. Server-side persistence (server action/route) auth-gated seller/admin. Data model shaped so an automated quoter can later populate the SAME field with no migration. New nullable field e.g. ApparelListing.usLandedCost as integer cents USD — schema change, prisma db push to both DBs. Auth mocked via vi.mock(). Test round-trip + that it never enters a pricing path. CORRECTION (founder, post-merge): the cost is ADMIN-set and SELLER view-only (was seller-editable) — reflects the architectural seller/admin split. The write moved out of the seller's updateReferencedListingAction (which now ignores any submitted usLandedCost) into an ADMIN-gated setUsLandedCostAction (src/app/actions/us-landed-cost.ts); the seller edit form shows the value+band read-only; the admin Teemill catalog view gets an inline editor (UsLandedCostEditor). Tests updated: admin round-trip/clear/negative/non-admin + seller-update-ignores-cost.

stories.notes: Depends on 19.5. Show recorded US-landed cost (USD) on BOTH the admin Teemill catalog/audit view AND the seller listing-creation picker; clear 'not recorded' state when null. Number AND a display-only color band (green/amber/red) derived from two ADMIN-configurable thresholds, rendered as a badge — band is presentational, recomputed from the stored number, no label state persisted; sellers see both number + band at a glance. Not-recorded => neutral badge, not a misleading band. Both surfaces read the same value from 19.5 — single source of truth. Admin view supports sort/group by US-landed cost (cheapest-first scan). Thresholds stored as two config values (amber-above, red-above), not per-product. Test band derivation as a pure fn over (cost, thresholds) incl null->'not recorded'; component tests both surfaces for recorded/not-recorded/each band. Buyer-facing surfaces untouched — confirm no cost-data leak to buyer views.

stories.epic: Epic MFTF-19: Storefront Polish & Pricing Visibility
stories.title: Seller Selects a Background Color per Apparel Mockup
stories.notes: For referenced (Teemill) listings whose mockups arrive transparent, seller assigns a background to EACH mockup from picker swatches: white, black, three greys between. STORED AS A COLOR VALUE (e.g. hex string), NOT a fixed enum — so future colors (or later background images) need no migration and don't disturb existing listings. Picker DEFINES/offers the five current swatches as a UI-layer constant; RENDERER reads the stored color value and treats it as opaque (renders any valid value, assumes nothing about the five-set). Unset => defined default (e.g. white) at render. Background composited BEHIND the transparent mockup at RENDER TIME (CSS background behind ) on buyer carousel/detail + seller preview — stored image NEVER modified. Applies wherever the mockup renders (detail carousel + derived thumbnails). Auth-gated to listing seller/admin; persists across sessions. Store as nullable string color per mockup (NOT a Prisma enum) — the deliberate open-ended seam. If Teemill mockups are held only as cached URLs on the referenced-variant snapshot, needs a small persisted structure to attach per-mockup background (mockupBackgrounds map keyed by mockup identity vs child row) — flag shape in handoff. Distinct from Epic MFTF-PF PrintSizeMockup (seller-UPLOADED print mockups); this is background selection on provider-served apparel mockups. Test: arbitrary stored color renders (picker/renderer share no enum coupling); default-when-unset; round-trip. STORAGE DECISION (founder-confirmed): nullable Json ApparelListing.mockupBackgrounds keyed by colorName (mockups are one-per-colour; survives re-sync, which deletes/recreates ReferencedVariant rows). NOT a Prisma enum. Pure resolver in src/lib/apparel/mockup-background.ts (DEFAULT #ffffff, 5-swatch UI constant: white/black/3 greys); opaque renderer composites CSS background behind on buyer carousel+thumbnails (ApparelProductView via ApparelDetailImage.backgroundColor) and seller preview (ReferencedImageCarousel + MockupBackgroundPicker); setMockupBackgroundAction auth-gated to the listing's seller. Schema pushed to both DBs (no new table).
